question: Which of the following describes the storage of data in Snowflake tables?

options.explanation: Snowflake stores data in proprietary columnar format.

overall_explanation: Snowflake organizes data into micro-partitions. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/tables-micro-partitions

question: An administrator wants to prevent a specific warehouse from exceeding a monthly budget. They set up a Resource Monitor with a hard limit of 1000 credits. What happens when the 'Hard Suspend' threshold is reached?

options.text: The warehouse suspends after all current queries finish.
The warehouse continues but no new queries are accepted.
The warehouse suspends immediately and all currently running queries are canceled.
The warehouse size is automatically reduced.
options.explanation: Snowflake does not auto-resize size down this way.

overall_explanation: Hard Suspend cancels all running queries and stops the warehouse immediately to stop credit burn. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/resource-monitors

question: A user wants to check the historical credit usage for the 'Search Optimization Service' and 'Automatic Clustering'. Which view provides this specific information?

options.explanation: Shows credits for all serverless features including Search Optimization and Clustering.

overall_explanation: METERING_DAILY_HISTORY in Account Usage tracks credit consumption for all background serverless services. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/sql-reference/account-usage/metering_daily_history

question: An Administrator wants to ensure that specific users can only access Snowflake from the corporate office IP range. Which object should be implemented?
overall_explanation: Network policies allow you to restrict access based on IP address ranges. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/network-policies

question: An administrator needs to identify which queries are consuming the most credits from the last 45 minutes. Which view should they use for the most up-to-date information?

options.explanation: Latency of up to 45 mins - 3 hours.
Shows load not specific query credits.
Correct. Information Schema provides real-time data for recent queries.

overall_explanation: Information Schema is the go-to for real-time monitoring of the last 7 days. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/sql-reference/info-schema/query_history

question: An organization wants to share a secure view with a consumer. They want to ensure the consumer cannot see the underlying SQL definition of the view. What must they use?
overall_explanation: Secure Views hide the DDL and optimization internals from the users who have access to them. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/views-secure"

question: Which of the following conditions would prevent Snowflake from effectively using Query Pruning to optimize performance?

options.text: Applying a function to a filtered column in the WHERE clause (e.g. WHERE UPPER(column) = VALUE).
options.explanation: Functions on columns often prevent the optimizer from using metadata for pruning.
Pruning is designed for millions of partitions.

options.text: Using a very large Virtual Warehouse.
options.explanation: Warehouse size does not affect pruning logic.

options.text: Using a WHERE clause on a clustered column.

overall_explanation: Pruning relies on min/max metadata; applying functions to columns often breaks this optimization. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/tables-clustering-micro-partitions

question: Which of the following are FALSE regarding Snowflake Micro-partitions? (Select all that apply)

options.text: They are stored in a row-based format.

options.explanation: True. They cannot be changed once written.

options.text: They are approximately 50 MB to 500 MB of data.

overall_explanation: Snowflake automatically manages immutable columnar micro-partitions. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/tables-micro-partitions

question: Which of the following describes the behavior of a 'Standard' Scaling Policy in a Multi-cluster Warehouse?

options.text: It starts new clusters immediately as soon as a query is queued.
It prevents any queuing of queries.
options.explanation: Queuing can still happen during startup.

options.text: It only scales up the size of the nodes.
It waits 6 minutes before starting a new cluster.

overall_explanation: The Standard policy is designed to minimize queuing by starting clusters as fast as possible. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/warehouses-multicluster

question: Which command should be used to change the size of an existing Virtual Warehouse? SET WAREHOUSE_SIZE MODIFY WAREHOUSE UPDATE WAREHOUSE
overall_explanation: ALTER WAREHOUSE is used to change properties like size, auto-suspend and max_cluster_count. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/sql-reference/sql/alter-warehouse

question: Which of the following are required to create an External Table in Snowflake?

options.text: A Primary Key defined on the source files

overall_explanation: External tables require a stage and proper cloud permissions (Integration). Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/tables-external-intro

question: Which role is best suited for managing 'Network Policies' and 'Grants' but does not necessarily need access to data?

options.explanation: Correct. Focused on security management. ACCOUNTADMIN USERADMIN SYSADMIN

overall_explanation: SECURITYADMIN focuses on RBAC and security object management. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/security-access-control-overview

question: Which role should be used to monitor account-level credit usage and billing?
overall_explanation: ACCOUNTADMIN is the primary role for financial and account-level oversight. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/security-access-control-overview"

question: In a Snowflake account with Account Usage and Information Schema, which of the following is a key difference between them?

options.text: Account Usage only exists in the Standard Edition.
Information Schema retains data for 1 year.

options.explanation: Only Account Usage has dropped objects.

options.text: Account Usage latency can be up to 3 hours while Information Schema is real-time.

overall_explanation: Account Usage is for long-term auditing; Information Schema is for immediate metadata needs. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/sql-reference/account-usage

question: When using the 'Search Optimization Service' which type of queries benefit the most?

options.text: Large table scans with no filters.
Point lookups on high-cardinality columns (e.g. searching for a specific ID).

overall_explanation: Search Optimization speeds up point lookups in large tables with millions of rows. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/search-optimization-service

question: Which Snowflake feature allows for the automatic execution of SQL statements on a scheduled basis?
overall_explanation: Tasks are used to schedule SQL or Stored Procedures based on a cron or interval. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/tasks-intro"

question: Which command is used to unload data from a Snowflake table into a Stage?
overall_explanation: The COPY INTO command is used to export data. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/data-export-intro

question: In Snowflake, who is the owner of an object by default?

options.text: The person who paid for the account.
The role that was used to create the object.

overall_explanation: Snowflake uses Role-Based Access Control where the creating role owns the object. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/security-access-control-overview"

question: An organization wants to share data with a Consumer but needs to restrict specific rows based on the Consumer's region. Which feature should they use?

options.explanation: Correct. Allows filtering rows based on attributes or roles.

overall_explanation: Row Access Policies are designed to implement fine-grained data security at the row level. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/security-row

question: Which command is used to see the description and data types of all columns in a specific table?
overall_explanation: DESCRIBE (or DESC) returns the definition of an object's columns and types. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/sql-reference/sql/desc-table

question: What is the main advantage of using 'External Tables'?

options.text: They are faster than internal tables.
They provide 90 days of Time Travel.
options.explanation: External tables have no Time Travel.

options.text: They allow you to query data without first importing it into Snowflake.
They do not require a Virtual Warehouse.
options.explanation: They do require one for queries.

overall_explanation: External tables provide a way to access a data lake without ingestion. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/tables-external-intro"

question: Which of the following are types of caching available in Snowflake?

options.explanation: Snowflake does not cache external stages.

overall_explanation: Snowflake utilizes Result Cache and Local Disk Cache. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/performance-query-caching

question: Which metadata is stored for each column in a micro-partition to enable 'Pruning'?

options.text: The minimum and maximum values in that partition.
options.explanation: Correct. Used by the optimizer to skip partitions.

overall_explanation: The optimizer checks the min/max values of each partition against the query filters to skip irrelevant data. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/tables-micro-partitions

question: Which of the following is NOT a type of 'Stage' in Snowflake? User Stage

options.explanation: Correct. This does not exist. Named Internal Stage Table Stage

overall_explanation: Stages are categorized as User, Table or Named (Internal/External). Warehouses do not have stages. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/data-load-local-file-system-create-stage

question: True or False: Snowflake charges a separate fee for the Cloud Services layer if it exceeds 10% of the daily warehouse credit consumption.

options.explanation: There is a cost if it exceeds the 10% allowance.

overall_explanation: Users only pay for Cloud Services if the credits used exceed 10% of their total daily warehouse usage. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/cost-understanding-overall

question: A company has a multi-cluster warehouse set to Auto-scale mode with a minimum of 1 cluster and a maximum of 5. The scaling policy is set to Economy. What is the primary behavior of this configuration?

options.text: It scales up the size of the warehouse.
options.explanation: Warehouses scale out (clusters) not up (size) automatically.

options.text: It prioritizes cost savings by only starting a new cluster if the system estimates there is enough load to keep it busy for 6 minutes.
It starts a new cluster immediately when a query is queued.
It maintains all 5 clusters active during business hours.

overall_explanation: The Economy policy focuses on throughput and credit savings rather than immediate sub-second starts. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/warehouses-multicluster

question: When data is 'Unloaded' from Snowflake to a Stage using COPY INTO, what is the default compression used? snappy None (uncompressed) zip
overall_explanation: Snowflake defaults to gzip compression when unloading data to files in a stage. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/sql-reference/sql/copy-into-location

question: Which of the following statements are TRUE regarding Snowflake's Query Result Cache? (Select all that apply)

options.text: It is shared across all users in the account.
The cache is purged if the underlying data changes.
It requires an active Virtual Warehouse to be accessed.
options.explanation: False. Results are in Cloud Services.

overall_explanation: The result cache is managed by the Cloud Services layer and lasts 24 hours unless data changes. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/performance-query-caching

question: Which of the following is a FALSE statement regarding 'Fail-safe' in Snowflake?

options.text: It cannot be disabled or configured by the user.
It begins immediately after the Time Travel retention period ends.
It is available for both Permanent and Transient tables.
options.explanation: False. Transient and Temporary tables have no Fail-safe.

options.text: It provides a 7-day period for data recovery by Snowflake Support.

overall_explanation: Transient and Temporary tables are specifically designed to save costs by excluding Fail-safe. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/data-failsafe

question: Which view in the ACCOUNT_USAGE schema would you query to find the credits consumed by the 'Cloud Services' layer specifically? STORAGE_USAGE WAREHOUSE_METERING_HISTORY

options.explanation: Correct. Tracks warehouse and serverless/cloud services credits. QUERY_HISTORY

overall_explanation: METERING_DAILY_HISTORY provides a breakdown of all credit-consuming services in the account. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/sql-reference/account-usage/metering_daily_history

question: An analyst needs to query a JSON file in an S3 bucket without loading it into a Snowflake table. What is the most efficient way?

options.text: Create an External Table over the S3 stage.
options.explanation: Allows querying data directly in place.

options.text: Load it into a VARIANT column first.

overall_explanation: External tables allow querying files in cloud storage using SQL without ingestion. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/tables-external-intro

question: A company is loading files from a stage where filenames include a 'load_date'. How can they include this date into a table column during the COPY INTO process?

options.text: By renaming the files to match the columns.
By using a SELECT statement with METADATA$FILENAME within the COPY command.
options.explanation: Correct. Transformations are allowed during load.

options.text: It is impossible to get metadata during load.

overall_explanation: Snowflake allows querying metadata like filename and row number during the ingestion process. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/querying-metadata"

question: A Consumer account is accessing a Share from a Provider. The Provider updates the underlying table data. When can the Consumer see the updated data?

options.explanation: Correct. Shared data is live and real-time.

options.text: After the Provider refreshes the Share metadata.

options.explanation: Cache doesn't prevent seeing live updates.

overall_explanation: Data Sharing in Snowflake is live; there is no data movement or copying required to see updates. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/data-sharing-intro

question: Which features are ONLY available in the Enterprise Edition or higher? (Select all that apply)

options.text: Up to 90 days of Time Travel

overall_explanation: Standard edition is limited to 1-day time travel and lacks advanced performance features. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/intro-editions

question: Which statement is TRUE regarding Materialized Views in Snowflake?

options.explanation: They incur both storage and compute costs.

options.text: They require a background service that consumes credits for maintenance.
options.explanation: Correct. Snowflake automatically maintains them when base tables change.

options.text: They are available in the Standard edition.
They can be created on top of other Materialized Views.

overall_explanation: Materialized Views are serverless objects that incur maintenance costs to stay synchronized. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/views-materialized

question: A customer is using Snowpipe to load data. They notice files are not being loaded and use SYSTEM$PIPE_STATUS. The status is 'STALLED_COMPILATION'. What does this likely indicate?

options.text: The SQL in the pipe definition is invalid or there is a schema mismatch.
options.explanation: Indicates a failure in compiling the load instructions.

overall_explanation: STALLED_COMPILATION usually points to an error in the COPY statement within the pipe. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/sql-reference/functions/system_pipe_status

question: A Business Critical account user notices that data recovery is needed for a table dropped 10 days ago. The retention period was set to 1 day. What is the status of this data?

options.text: It is in the Cloud Services cache.
It is in Fail-safe and can only be recovered by Snowflake Support.
options.explanation: Correct. Fail-safe lasts 7 days after Time Travel ends (1+7=8). Wait - 10 days is beyond both.
Correct. 1 day (TT) + 7 days (FS) = 8 days total protection. Day 10 is too late.

overall_explanation: Data is only recoverable for the sum of Time Travel plus Fail-safe days (max 97 days). Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/data-failsafe

question: Which role is recommended for creating and managing users and roles in a Snowflake account?
overall_explanation: USERADMIN is dedicated to creating and managing users and roles. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/security-access-control-overview

question: Which function is used to flatten a VARIANT column that contains a list of objects? EXTRACT()
overall_explanation: FLATTEN is the specific table function used for lateral joins to explode arrays. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/sql-reference/functions/flatten

question: Which of the following are benefits of using the Search Optimization Service? (Select all that apply)

options.text: Speeds up small point-lookup queries on large tables.
Improves the performance of large table scans.
options.explanation: False. It is for point lookups.

options.text: Reduces the need for manual clustering.

overall_explanation: Search Optimization is an auxiliary data structure that improves point lookup performance. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/search-optimization-service

question: When loading data using 'Snowpipe', how is the cost calculated?

options.text: Based on the size of the Virtual Warehouse.
Based on the number of users in the account.
Based on a flat monthly fee.
Based on the number of files loaded and a per-second compute charge.

overall_explanation: Snowpipe charging is based on file processing and a small overhead for management. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/data-load-snowpipe-billing"

question: What is the maximum Time Travel retention period for a Permanent table in Snowflake Enterprise Edition?
overall_explanation: Enterprise Edition allows up to 90 days of Time Travel. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/data-time-travel

question: A 'Standard' Multi-cluster Warehouse is set to Min Clusters: 2, Max Clusters: 2. What is this mode called? Economy Auto-scale

options.explanation: Correct. Both numbers are the same. Standard

overall_explanation: Maximized mode starts all configured clusters immediately when the warehouse is started. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/warehouses-multicluster

question: In Snowflake, 'Clustering Depth' of 1.0 means what?

options.explanation: Correct. Indicates no overlapping micro-partitions for the key.

overall_explanation: A clustering depth of 1 means that for any value of the clustering key, only one micro-partition needs to be scanned. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/tables-clustering-micro-partitions

question: A user is executing a complex JOIN across three large tables and the Query Profile shows Local Disk Spilling. What is the most effective way to resolve this?

options.text: Increase the Virtual Warehouse size (e.g. Small to Large).
options.explanation: Spilling occurs when RAM is insufficient; a larger WH provides more RAM/SSD.
Retention does not affect execution RAM.

options.text: Create a Cluster Key on all three tables.
options.explanation: Might help but does not fix RAM limits.

overall_explanation: Disk spilling happens when data exceeds the Warehouse memory. Upsizing the WH is the standard solution. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/performance-query-warehouse-size

question: What happens to the data in a 'Temporary Table' if the user's session is disconnected due to a network failure?

options.text: The table and all its data are dropped immediately.
options.explanation: Correct. Temporary tables only exist for the duration of the session.

options.text: The data is moved to Fail-safe.
The table is converted to a Transient table.
The data is stored for 24 hours of Time Travel.
options.explanation: Time Travel is possible only if the session is active.

overall_explanation: Temporary tables are non-persistent and are purged when the session ends. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/tables-temp-transient"

question: What is the maximum file size recommended for a single file when loading data into Snowflake for optimal performance?
overall_explanation: Snowflake recommends splitting large files into the 100-250MB range for parallel processing. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/data-load-considerations-prepare"

question: Which role is the only one capable of managing Snowflake's 'Organization' level features such as creating new accounts?
overall_explanation: ORGADMIN is a special role for managing multiple Snowflake accounts and organization settings. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/admin-user-management

question: What is the impact of using 'ORDER BY' in a view definition on the underlying table's clustering?

options.text: It improves pruning for all queries.
options.explanation: Only for queries using that view.

options.text: It has no impact on the underlying table's physical clustering.
options.explanation: Correct. Clustering is a physical property; views are logical.

options.text: It speeds up the next load.

overall_explanation: Views do not change how data is physically stored on disk. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/views-introduction

question: Which background service is responsible for automatically rearranging data in micro-partitions to match a table's clustering key?
overall_explanation: Automatic Clustering is a serverless service that manages the data layout based on defined keys. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/tables-clustering-micro-partitions

question: A user executes: SELECT * FROM my_table AT(OFFSET => -60*5). What are they attempting to do?

options.text: Query the state of the table as it was 5 minutes ago using Time Travel.
options.explanation: Correct. OFFSET calculates time backwards in seconds.

options.text: Create a clone of the table.

overall_explanation: The AT clause allows Time Travel queries based on a specific timestamp or offset. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/data-time-travel

question: A company is migrating from a legacy system and has JSON files with nested arrays. They want to flatten this data into a relational format. Which SQL operator is required?

options.explanation: Used to convert string to variant.
Correct. Used to explode arrays into multiple rows.

overall_explanation: The FLATTEN function is used to convert semi-structured data (arrays/objects) into multiple rows. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/sql-reference/functions/flatten

question: What is the purpose of the 'VALIDATION_MODE' parameter in a COPY INTO statement?

options.text: To speed up the load process.
options.explanation: It slows it down because it doesn't load.

options.text: To parse the files and check for errors without actually loading the data.
To verify the checksum of the S3 bucket.

overall_explanation: Validation mode is used to test data quality before performing a bulk load. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/sql-reference/sql/copy-into-table

question: A company requires HIPAA compliance and PHI data encryption. Which is the minimum Snowflake edition they must subscribe to?

options.explanation: Includes 90-day time travel but not HIPAA by default.
Does not support Business Critical security.
Highest security tier for compliance like HIPAA/HITRUST.

overall_explanation: Business Critical edition is required for compliance standards like HIPAA and higher levels of encryption. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/intro-editions

question: Which of the following describes 'Secondary Roles' in Snowflake?

options.text: Allows a user to use the aggregate permissions of multiple roles in a single session.
options.explanation: Correct. Introduced to simplify permission management.

options.text: A role inherited by the PUBLIC role.
A way to assign a user to a backup role.
A role that can only read data.

overall_explanation: Secondary roles allow users to perform actions without constantly switching their active primary role. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/security-access-control-overview"

question: Which of the following describes 'Search Optimization'?

options.text: A tool for full-text search in PDF files.
A persistent data structure that acts like a secondary index to speed up point lookups.
A feature of the Standard edition.

overall_explanation: Search Optimization is a serverless feature for improving needle-in-a-haystack queries. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/search-optimization-service

question: Which privilege allows a role to see the query profile and execution details of queries run by other users?
overall_explanation: The MONITOR privilege on a warehouse allows a user to see all activity on that compute resource. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/security-access-control-privileges

question: A user wants to upload a file from their local laptop to an internal Snowflake stage. Which tool/command MUST they use?
overall_explanation: The PUT command is used to upload local files and requires a client like SnowSQL or a driver. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/data-load-local-file-system-upload"

question: Which privilege is required for a role to be able to use a Virtual Warehouse?
overall_explanation: The USAGE privilege allows a role to submit queries to a warehouse. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/security-access-control-privileges"

question: What is the maximum number of days the 'QUERY_HISTORY' view in the 'INFORMATION_SCHEMA' can return? 365 days
overall_explanation: Information Schema is limited to 7 days. Use Account Usage for longer periods. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/sql-reference/info-schema/query_history

question: Which component of the Cloud Services layer prevents two users from modifying the same data at the exact same millisecond?
overall_explanation: Transaction Management ensures ACID compliance and handles locking and concurrency control. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/intro-key-concepts"

question: True or False: A 'Task' in Snowflake can be configured to run only if a specific 'Stream' contains new changed data.

options.explanation: Correct. Using the WHEN SYSTEM$STREAM_HAS_DATA function.
This is a common use case.

overall_explanation: Tasks can be conditionally executed based on the presence of data in a stream to save credits. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/tasks-intro

question: Which function is used to convert a JSON string into a Snowflake VARIANT data type?
overall_explanation: PARSE_JSON takes a string and turns it into a structured VARIANT object. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/sql-reference/functions/parse_json"

question: Which of the following metadata is NOT tracked by Snowflake for each micro-partition?

options.text: The range of values (min/max) for each column.
The names of the users who queried the partition.
The total number of rows in the partition.

overall_explanation: User query history is tracked in separate logs not in the storage metadata. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/tables-micro-partitions"

question: What is the effect of the 'DATA_RETENTION_TIME_IN_DAYS' parameter being set to 0 for a Permanent table?

options.text: Time Travel is disabled for that table.
The table becomes a Temporary table.

overall_explanation: Setting retention to 0 effectively turns off the ability to use Time Travel for that object. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/data-time-travel"

question: A query shows 'Remote Disk Spilling' in the Query Profile. Which action would be the most effective to improve performance?

options.explanation: Helps pruning but not memory overflow.

options.text: Increase the size of the Virtual Warehouse.
options.explanation: Provides more local SSD and RAM to prevent remote spilling.
Used for point lookups not joins/spilling.
Adds concurrency but not more RAM per node.

overall_explanation: Remote disk spilling is the slowest overflow level. Increasing warehouse size adds memory and local SSD. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/performance-query-warehouse-size

question: What is the primary impact of 'Overlapping Micro-partitions' on query performance?

options.text: It decreases the effectiveness of query pruning.
options.explanation: Correct. High overlap means more partitions must be scanned.

overall_explanation: When micro-partitions overlap significantly for a specific column. Snowflake cannot skip them effectively. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/tables-micro-partitions

question: Which of the following are TRUE regarding 'Internal Stages' in Snowflake? (Select all that apply)

options.text: There are named stages created within Snowflake.
They use the same storage pricing as tables.
Users are charged for data storage in internal stages.
They are always public to the internet.
options.explanation: False. They are secure and managed by Snowflake.

overall_explanation: Internal stages are managed by Snowflake and follow the same storage cost as data in tables. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/data-load-local-file-system-create-stage"

question: A security requirement mandates that a Social Security Number column must be visible only to the HR_MANAGER role. Which feature should be used?

options.explanation: Correct for column-level security based on roles.

overall_explanation: Masking policies allow selective visibility of sensitive data based on the active role. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/security-column-ddm-intro

question: A financial firm needs to share data with a partner who does NOT have a Snowflake account. What should the firm create?
overall_explanation: Reader accounts are managed by the provider for consumers without their own Snowflake instance. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/data-sharing-reader-create

question: If a transaction is left open and the session is terminated, what is the default behavior in Snowflake?

options.text: The transaction stays open for 24 hours.
The data is moved to a temporary table.

options.explanation: Correct. Snowflake ensures ACID compliance by rolling back uncommitted work.

overall_explanation: Snowflake automatically rolls back transactions from disconnected or failed sessions. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/sql-reference/transactions

question: What is the 'Fail-safe' duration for a table in the Snowflake Standard Edition?
overall_explanation: Fail-safe is always 7 days for permanent tables regardless of the edition. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/data-failsafe"

question: A Data Engineer notices that Snowpipe is not loading data but the files are present in the S3 bucket. Which Information Schema table function should be used to see if Snowflake even detected the files?

options.explanation: Not a standard function for detection.
Shows historical loads but not detection of new files.

overall_explanation: The COPY_HISTORY function (or view) shows the status of files loaded or failed in the last 14 days. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/sql-reference/functions/copy_history

question: Which of the following commands will remove all data from a table but keep the table structure?
overall_explanation: TRUNCATE TABLE removes all rows and is a DDL operation. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/sql-reference/sql/truncate-table"

question: Which of the following are TRUE regarding the differences between INFORMATION_SCHEMA and the ACCOUNT_USAGE share? (Select all that apply)

options.text: Account Usage has a data latency of 45 minutes to 3 hours.
Information Schema includes objects that have been dropped.
options.explanation: False. Only Account Usage includes dropped objects.

options.text: Account Usage retention is typically 365 days.
Information Schema is a set of views provided for each database.

overall_explanation: Account Usage is for long-term auditing with latency while Information Schema is for real-time metadata. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/sql-reference/account-usage#differences-between-account-usage-and-information-schema

question: A company needs to audit all failed login attempts across their entire Snowflake account from the last 6 months. Which view should they query?

options.explanation: Real-time but only retains up to 7 days.
Correct. Retains data for up to 1 year and covers the entire account.

overall_explanation: Account Usage views retain historical data for 365 days while Information Schema is for recent data. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/sql-reference/account-usage/login_history

question: A user needs to load data continuously from an S3 bucket as soon as files arrive. Which feature should they use?
overall_explanation: Snowpipe uses a serverless compute model. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/data-load-snowpipe-intro

question: Which of the following describes the 'Clustering Depth' of a table in Snowflake?

options.text: The time it takes to re-cluster a table.
The total number of micro-partitions in a table.
A measure of how well the table is partitioned; a smaller depth indicates better clustering.
options.explanation: Correct. Low depth means fewer micro-partitions overlap for specific values.

options.text: The number of columns defined in a cluster key.

overall_explanation: Clustering depth helps determine if a table needs a cluster key or if current pruning is efficient. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/tables-clustering-micro-partitions#clustering-depth

question: A Virtual Warehouse is running 100 queries simultaneously. The 'Queued Overload' metric is high. What is the best way to handle this 'Concurrency' issue?

options.text: Increase the size from Small to Large.
options.explanation: Increases speed of single queries but not necessarily concurrency capacity.

options.text: Add more clusters to a Multi-cluster warehouse.
options.explanation: Correct. Adding clusters handles more simultaneous queries (Scale-out).
Clears cache and slows down queries.

overall_explanation: Scale-out (adding clusters) is the solution for concurrency; Scale-up (increasing size) is for complex slow queries. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/warehouses-multicluster"

question: A provider wants to share data with multiple consumers via a 'Data Exchange'. Which of the following is TRUE?

options.text: It allows the provider to control who can join the exchange and see the data listings.
options.explanation: Correct. It is a private marketplace.

options.text: Consumers are always charged for the storage of shared data.
Data is copied to each consumer's account.
It is only available for Business Critical accounts.

overall_explanation: A Data Exchange is a private circle for sharing data securely with specific invited members. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/data-exchange-intro

question: How many 'Reader Accounts' can a Snowflake Provider account create?
overall_explanation: Providers can create as many reader accounts as needed to support their consumers. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/data-sharing-reader-create

question: Which of the following describes the 'Security' of data at rest in Snowflake?

options.text: All data is automatically encrypted using AES-256.
Users must manually encrypt their data.
Encryption is only available for Business Critical.
Only Enterprise edition encrypts data at rest.

overall_explanation: Snowflake provides transparent data encryption for all customers at no extra cost. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/security-encryption"

question: True or False: A single Snowflake account can span across multiple Cloud Providers (e.g., AWS and Azure). TRUE
overall_explanation: An account is tied to a specific region and provider at creation. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/intro-key-concepts"

question: True or False: Snowflake's architecture allows for simultaneous 'Loading' and 'Querying' of the same table without contention.

options.explanation: Correct. Due to multi-version concurrency control (MVCC).

overall_explanation: Snowflake's architecture ensures that readers do not block writers and vice versa. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/intro-key-concepts

question: A company wants to share data with a consumer via a Reader Account. Who pays for the compute credits used by that Reader Account?
overall_explanation: Reader accounts are paid for by the provider account that created them. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/data-sharing-reader-create"

question: A company wants to track the 'Data Lineage' to see which downstream views are affected by a table change. Which Account Usage view is most relevant? ACCESS_HISTORY DATA_TRANSFER_HISTORY TABLE_STORAGE_METRICS

options.explanation: Correct. Shows the relationship between tables and views.

overall_explanation: OBJECT_DEPENDENCIES tracks how one object (like a view) refers to another (like a table). Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/object-dependencies

question: Which command will show the amount of storage credits used by 'Fail-safe' data across the entire account?

options.explanation: Correct. Contains columns for Fail-safe bytes. DESCRIBE ACCOUNT SHOW STORAGE SHOW FAILSAFE

overall_explanation: TABLE_STORAGE_METRICS provides a detailed breakdown of Time Travel and Fail-safe storage usage. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/sql-reference/account-usage/table_storage_metrics

question: A company has 50 TB of data and wants to implement 'Access History' to see which columns are most frequently queried for GDPR compliance. Which Snowflake edition is the minimum required?

options.explanation: Account Usage Access History is an Enterprise feature (and above).
Correct. Access History is part of the Snowflake Governance package in Enterprise and higher.

overall_explanation: Access History is part of the advanced governance features available in Enterprise Edition and above. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/access-history

question: Which feature allows you to 'undo' a DROP TABLE command within the Time Travel period?

options.explanation: Correct. RECOVER TABLE CLONE TABLE RESTORE TABLE

overall_explanation: UNDROP restores the last version of a dropped object as long as it is within the Time Travel window. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/sql-reference/sql/undrop-table

question: Which type of table is most appropriate for storing large amounts of transient data that does not require Fail-safe protection but should persist across multiple sessions?

options.explanation: Persists across sessions but lacks Fail-safe.

overall_explanation: Transient tables are ideal for ETL work-stages to save on Fail-safe storage costs. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/tables-temp-transient

question: Which of the following commands can be used to see the credit usage for a specific 'Task'? MONITOR TASK DESCRIBE TASK
overall_explanation: Serverless features like Tasks have specific history functions to track their consumption. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/sql-reference/functions/serverless_task_history

question: What is the specific purpose of the System-defined Function SYSTEM$PIPE_STATUS?

options.text: To monitor the health and status of a Snowpipe.
To check the syntax of a COPY INTO statement.
To refresh an external stage manually.
To see the credits consumed by a Warehouse.

overall_explanation: This function is critical for diagnosing data ingestion issues in continuous pipelines. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/sql-reference/functions/system_pipe_status

question: Which Snowflake object allows you to store and query files in a cloud location without ever moving the data into Snowflake storage?

options.explanation: Correct. It maps metadata over external files. Internal Stage Materialized View Transient Table

overall_explanation: External tables act as a schema-on-read layer over data lakes like S3 or Azure Blob. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/tables-external-intro

question: Which function allows you to extract all the keys present in a VARIANT column containing a JSON object? GET_KEYS() FLATTEN()
overall_explanation: OBJECT_KEYS returns an array of the top-level keys in a VARIANT object. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/sql-reference/functions/object_keys

question: Which background service is used to optimize the storage of semi-structured data by 'sub-columnarizing' VARIANT columns? The Metadata Manager

options.explanation: Correct. This is handled automatically during ingestion. The Optimizer Automatic Clustering

overall_explanation: Snowflake automatically extracts common fields from JSON/VARIANT into a columnar format for faster access. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/tables-micro-partitions

question: Which of the following are TRUE regarding 'Snowflake Managed Keys'? (Select all that apply)

options.text: They are used for transparent data encryption.
They are based on a hierarchical key model.
Users must manually create them before loading data.

overall_explanation: Snowflake manages a multi-level hierarchy of keys that are automatically rotated. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/security-encryption

question: If a 'Resource Monitor' is set to 'Notify' at 80% and 'Suspend' at 100%, what happens if the usage reaches 85%?

options.text: An alert is sent but the warehouse continues to run.

overall_explanation: A Notify action only triggers an alert to the designated administrators. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/resource-monitors

question: Which type of Snowflake role is required to create a 'Share' object? SECURITYADMIN SYSADMIN USERADMIN

options.explanation: Correct (or role with specific global privilege).

overall_explanation: Creating a share is a high-level administrative task usually reserved for ACCOUNTADMIN. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/data-sharing-intro

question: Which view would you use to find out which 'Network Policies' are currently assigned to the account? LOGIN_HISTORY

options.explanation: Shows existing policies but not their assignment.
Correct. General view for all policy assignments. ACCOUNT_PARAMETERS

overall_explanation: The POLICY_REFERENCES view shows which policies are active on which objects/account. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/sql-reference/account-usage/policy_references

question: Which Snowflake layer is responsible for query optimization and transaction management?

options.explanation: Correct layer for metadata and optimization.

overall_explanation: The Cloud Services layer is the brain of Snowflake. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/intro-key-concepts

question: A DevOps engineer needs to clone a production database to a development environment. The production database has 10 TB of data. What is the impact on storage costs immediately after cloning?

options.text: Storage costs are waived for the first 24 hours.
options.explanation: There is no such grace period.

options.text: Costs increase by 10% for the metadata overhead.
No additional storage costs are incurred for the data until the cloned objects are modified.
options.explanation: Correct. Initial cloning only creates metadata.

overall_explanation: Snowflake uses Zero-copy cloning which means data is shared between objects until changes occur. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/tables-storage-considerations

question: When using 'Dynamic Data Masking', what happens if a role without privileges tries to query a masked column?

options.text: The column is hidden from the result set.
options.explanation: Masking replaces values; it doesn't hide columns.

options.text: The user sees a masked value (e.g., ***** or NULL) based on the policy.
The query fails with an error.
The user sees the original data but a warning is logged.

overall_explanation: Masking policies intercept the data and replace it with a pre-defined mask for unauthorized roles. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/security-column-ddm-intro

question: Which type of data file format is NOT natively supported for data loading into Snowflake?
overall_explanation: Snowflake supports CSV, JSON, Parquet, Avro, ORC and XML. HTML is not a supported data load format. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/data-load-formats"

question: Which SQL function is used to convert a VARIANT column containing a date-string into a proper DATE type?
overall_explanation: You must first cast the variant to a string and then use a conversion function. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/querying-semistructured"

question: Which of the following describes 'Object Tagging'?

options.text: A feature for data governance that allows you to label objects (like sensitive columns) for tracking.
A way to speed up queries.

overall_explanation: Object tagging is a key component of Snowflake's data governance framework. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/object-tagging

question: In a retail scenario, you need to search for a specific 'Transaction ID' in a table with billions of rows. Which feature would most effectively speed up this specific 'point lookup'?

options.explanation: Helps, but SOS is faster for point lookups.
Correct. It acts like an index for equality searches in large tables.

overall_explanation: Search Optimization Service significantly reduces the time to find single rows in massive datasets. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/search-optimization-service

question: Which function is used to convert a JSON-formatted string into a VARIANT type?

options.explanation: Correct. Specifically validates and parses JSON strings.

overall_explanation: PARSE_JSON is the standard way to ingest semi-structured strings into a Snowflake-native format. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/sql-reference/functions/parse_json

question: A security admin wants to enforce MFA for all users in the account. Which statement is TRUE regarding Snowflake's MFA support?

options.text: MFA is powered by Duo Security and users must self-enroll via the Snowflake web interface.
MFA is only available in the Business Critical edition.
Snowflake manages its own MFA app.
MFA replaces the need for a password.

overall_explanation: Snowflake integrates with Duo Security to provide MFA across all editions at no extra cost. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/security-mfa

question: What is the 'Purge' option in the 'COPY INTO' command used for?

options.text: To drop the table if the load fails.
To delete the source files from the stage after they have been successfully loaded.
options.explanation: Correct. Helps manage stage storage costs.

options.text: To delete data from the table after loading.

overall_explanation: Using PURGE=TRUE is a best practice for keeping internal stages clean after data ingestion. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/sql-reference/sql/copy-into-table

question: Which of the following can be used as a 'source' for a 'Stream' in Snowflake?
overall_explanation: Streams can track changes on tables, directory tables, and even views. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/streams-intro

question: Which command is used to grant a role to a user?
overall_explanation: GRANT is the standard SQL command for managing permissions and role assignments in Snowflake. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/sql-reference/sql/grant-role

question: Which function is used to flatten a VARIANT that contains a 'Nested' object (not an array)?

options.explanation: Correct. (FLATTEN works for both arrays and objects).

overall_explanation: FLATTEN is the versatile tool for all semi-structured expansion needs. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/sql-reference/functions/flatten

question: Which feature should be used to provide a specific, masked version of data to a user based on their role?

options.explanation: Correct. Masks specific columns at query time.

overall_explanation: Dynamic Data Masking ensures sensitive data like SSNs are only seen by authorized roles. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/security-column-ddm-intro

question: Which of the following are benefits of 'Micro-partitions'? (Select all that apply)

options.text: They are automatically managed by Snowflake.
They allow for extremely efficient pruning.
They store data in a row-based format.

overall_explanation: Snowflake's columnar storage in micro-partitions is the key to its performance. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/tables-micro-partitions

question: What is the 'Query Profile' tool used for?

options.text: To analyze the execution plan and identify bottlenecks in a query.

overall_explanation: The Query Profile provides a visual breakdown of where time and resources were spent during a query. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/ui-query-profile

question: Which component of Snowflake manages 'Transactional Integrity' (ACID compliance)?

options.explanation: Correct. This layer manages all metadata and transactions.

overall_explanation: The Cloud Services layer acts as the coordinator, ensuring that all data changes are atomic and consistent. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/intro-key-concepts

question: Which component of the Snowflake architecture ensures that a query from User A does not see uncommitted changes from User B?

options.explanation: Correct. Handles ACID compliance and MVCC.

overall_explanation: Transaction management in the Cloud Services layer handles concurrency and state. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/intro-key-concepts

question: A user needs to access a file in a stage that must be valid for the duration of their specific session only. Which URL should be generated?

options.explanation: Correct. It is temporary and tied to the specific Snowflake session.
Can last longer than a session and is public.

overall_explanation: Scoped URLs are ideal for internal applications where access should expire with the user session. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/data-load-snowpipe-rest-files

question: True or False: Snowflake supports 'Snowpark' code execution in Python, Java, and Scala.
overall_explanation: Snowpark provides a DataFrame API for these languages, allowing developers to build complex pipelines. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/developer-guide/snowpark/index

question: Which of the following is TRUE about 'Micro-partitions'?

options.text: They are stored in the Cloud Services layer.

overall_explanation: Immutability is key to how Time Travel and Cloning work without data corruption. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/tables-micro-partitions

question: Which of the following is TRUE about 'Network Policies' and 'Public IP' addresses?

options.text: Network policies are only for the Snowflake UI.
Network policies can only allow single IPs, not ranges.
Network policies allow or block access based on the source IP of the request.

overall_explanation: Network policies provide the first line of defense at the network perimeter. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/network-policies

question: Which characteristic is unique to 'Transient' tables compared to 'Temporary' tables?

options.text: They are stored in the Cloud Services layer.
They support 90 days of Time Travel.
They persist across different sessions and can be seen by other users.

overall_explanation: Transient tables are for 'permanent but low-protection' data, whereas temporary are 'volatile'. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/tables-temp-transient

question: A user wants to clone a table that has 'Time Travel' data. Does the clone include the historical data of the original table?

options.text: Yes, but only if it's a 'Deep Clone'.
options.explanation: Plausible (standard in other DBs), but Snowflake only has Zero-copy cloning.

options.text: No, the clone only contains the data from the point in time it was created.
options.explanation: Correct. The clone starts its own new history.

options.text: Yes, the clone includes all historical data for the full retention period.
options.explanation: Plausible, but cloning only copies the metadata of the current state.

options.text: No, unless the CLONE_HISTORY parameter is TRUE.

overall_explanation: Cloning creates a new object; while it shares the base micro-partitions, its Time Travel lineage begins at creation. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/tables-storage-considerations

question: You are a Data Engineer at a retail company. During a massive sales event, the Marketing team reports that dashboards are slow. The Query Profile shows 'Queued Overload'. What is the most cost-effective solution?

options.explanation: This is for point-lookups, not concurrency.
This helps with aggregations, not overall warehouse queuing.

options.text: Add more clusters to a Multi-cluster warehouse (Scale-out).
options.explanation: Correct. Adding clusters handles more concurrent queries simultaneously.

options.text: Increase the Warehouse size from Small to Large.
options.explanation: This improves performance for complex queries but not concurrency.

overall_explanation: Scale-out (multi-cluster) is the standard solution for high concurrency issues. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/warehouses-multicluster

question: Which component of the 'Cloud Services' layer is responsible for choosing the most efficient way to execute a query (e.g., join order)?

options.explanation: Correct. Uses metadata to plan query execution.

overall_explanation: The Snowflake Optimizer is cost-based and leverages micro-partition metadata to plan queries. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/intro-key-concepts

question: A user wants to move a file from an Internal Stage back to their local laptop. Which command should they use?

options.explanation: Plausible, but GET is the SQL command.

overall_explanation: GET is the inverse of PUT and is executed via SnowSQL. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/sql-reference/sql/get

question: Which command is used to download files from an internal stage to your local computer?
overall_explanation: GET is the counterpart to PUT and is used for data export to local systems. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/sql-reference/sql/get

question: Which component of the Snowflake architecture is responsible for 'Query Optimization' and 'Access Control'?

options.explanation: Correct. This is the brain of Snowflake.

overall_explanation: The Cloud Services layer manages the entire system, including security, metadata, and optimization. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/intro-key-concepts

question: What is the default behavior of Snowflake when a 'Data Loading' error occurs and no error handling parameter is specified?

options.text: The load continues and ignores the error.
The data is moved to a 'DLQ' table.
The load fails and rolls back the entire operation.

overall_explanation: By default, Snowflake is strict and will stop the entire load if a single error is found. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/sql-reference/sql/copy-into-table

question: Which of the following is TRUE about the 'Result Cache'?

options.text: It is shared among all users in the account.
options.explanation: Correct. If two users run the same query, both can benefit.

options.text: It is stored in the Virtual Warehouse.
It is lost when the Warehouse is suspended.

overall_explanation: Result caching is global and helps reduce costs for repetitive queries across an entire organization. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/performance-query-caching

question: What happens to a 'Temporary Table' when the Snowflake session is closed?

options.text: It remains for 24 hours of Time Travel.
options.explanation: Access is lost immediately when the session ends.

options.text: It is converted to a Permanent table.
It is automatically dropped and the storage is purged.
options.explanation: Correct. Its lifecycle is tied to the session.

overall_explanation: Temporary tables are perfect for session-specific ETL work that does not need to persist. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/tables-temp-transient

question: You are a Data Engineer and a load fails with the error 'Numeric value not recognized'. Which tool or function would you use to find the exact line in the CSV that caused the error?

options.explanation: Shows the SQL but not the specific data error.
Shows the structure, not the data.
Correct. It returns details of errors from the last load.

overall_explanation: The VALIDATE function is the primary way to debug data quality issues during bulk loading. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/sql-reference/functions/validate

question: A company wants to share data with a consumer in a different Cloud Region (e.g., AWS US-East to AWS EU-West). What is required first?

options.text: Database Replication to the target region.
options.explanation: Correct. You can only share data that exists in the same region as the consumer.

overall_explanation: Snowflake Data Sharing is region-bound. Replication is the prerequisite for cross-region sharing. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/database-replication-intro

question: Which role is required to 'Create' a 'Share' for Data Sharing?
overall_explanation: Sharing involves account-to-account trust, so it is limited to the top role. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/data-sharing-intro

question: A retail company needs to allow a third-party vendor to download product images from a Snowflake internal stage without giving them a Snowflake login. Which URL type should be used?

options.explanation: Correct. It includes an access token and allows external access for a limited time.

overall_explanation: Pre-signed URLs are designed for sharing files with non-Snowflake users temporarily. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/data-load-snowpipe-rest-files

question: True or False: A 'Transient Table' can be converted into a 'Permanent Table' using an ALTER TABLE command.

options.explanation: Correct. The table type is set at creation and cannot be changed.

overall_explanation: To change a table type, you must create a new table (e.g., using CREATE TABLE ... AS SELECT). Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/tables-temp-transient

question: True or False: Snowflake allows you to use 'External Functions' to call code running in an AWS Lambda or Azure Function.
overall_explanation: External functions allow Snowflake to interact with external services for processing or data enrichment. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/external-functions-intro

question: A user runs: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM large_table (with no filters). Why does this query return instantly without a warehouse?

options.explanation: Result cache requires a previous execution.
Query applies to large tables too.

options.text: Because the Metadata Manager in Cloud Services stores the row count for every table.
options.explanation: Correct. Simple aggregates like COUNT, MIN, and MAX on the table level are stored in metadata.
Search optimization is for point lookups.

overall_explanation: The Metadata Manager tracks statistics for micro-partitions and tables, allowing certain queries to run 'warehouse-less'. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/intro-key-concepts

question: What is the primary purpose of 'SnowSQL'?

options.text: A command-line client for executing SQL and managing files (PUT/GET).
A graphical user interface for Snowflake.

overall_explanation: SnowSQL is the powerful CLI tool used for automation and local file management. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/snowsql

question: Which role is responsible for creating and managing 'Organization-wide' accounts and billing?

options.explanation: Correct. This role manages all accounts in a Snowflake organization.

overall_explanation: The ORGADMIN role allows for the creation of accounts across different cloud providers and regions. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/admin-user-management

question: Which of the following is a key difference between 'Information Schema' and 'Account Usage'?

options.text: Account Usage includes data for dropped objects.
options.explanation: Correct. Information Schema only shows current objects.

options.text: Account Usage is only for SYSADMIN.

overall_explanation: Account Usage is an audit-focused share with 365 days of history. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/sql-reference/account-usage

question: Which of the following is a supported 'Semi-structured' data type in Snowflake?

options.explanation: Not a separate type in Snowflake.

overall_explanation: Snowflake uses ARRAY, OBJECT, and the 'catch-all' VARIANT type for semi-structured data. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/sql-reference/data-types-semistructured

question: What is the default 'Minimum' billing time for a Virtual Warehouse when it is started?

options.explanation: Correct. After the first minute, it is per-second.

overall_explanation: Snowflake has a 60-second minimum charge to discourage frequent start/stop cycles that don't add value. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/cost-understanding-compute

question: Which command is used to assign a specific role to a user in Snowflake?
overall_explanation: Snowflake follows standard SQL syntax for granting roles to users. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/sql-reference/sql/grant-role

question: You notice 'Local Disk Spilling' in a Query Profile for a retail inventory report. What is the immediate recommendation?

options.text: Increase the size of the Virtual Warehouse.
options.explanation: Correct. Spilling means the warehouse RAM/SSD is full; a larger size provides more memory.
This helps with pruning but not with memory overflow.

options.text: Decrease the size of the Virtual Warehouse.
Turn on the Query Acceleration Service.
options.explanation: QAS helps with scans, not typically with join/agg spilling.

overall_explanation: Increasing warehouse size provides more local resources (RAM and SSD) to prevent data spilling to disk. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/performance-query-warehouse-size

question: A company needs to load data from Azure Blob Storage. Which 'Stage' type must they use?
overall_explanation: External stages are the bridge to cloud-native storage. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/data-load-azure

question: Which of the following is TRUE about Snowpark's DataFrame API?

options.text: It is always evaluated immediately (Eager evaluation).
It translates Python/Java/Scala code into SQL for execution.
options.explanation: Correct. It pushes the work to the warehouse.
It is excellent for JSON/Variant data.

options.text: It requires a Medium warehouse or larger.

overall_explanation: Snowpark DataFrames are evaluated lazily, meaning the query is only sent to Snowflake when an action is requested. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/developer-guide/snowpark/index

question: Which of the following is NOT a supported language for writing Snowflake Stored Procedures?
overall_explanation: Snowflake supports SQL, JavaScript, Python, Java, and Scala for stored procedures. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/sql-reference/stored-procedures-usage

question: Which scaling policy should be used if a company wants to minimize the number of clusters used, even if it means some queries have to wait?

options.explanation: Plausible name, but not a policy.

overall_explanation: Economy mode optimizes for cost by avoiding spinning up new clusters for short bursts. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/warehouses-multicluster

question: Which of the following describes 'Search Optimization Service' (SOS) behavior regarding DML?

options.text: Snowflake automatically manages and bills for background maintenance of search access paths.
SOS prevents any further DML until the index is updated.
SOS only works on static tables.
SOS must be manually refreshed after every INSERT.

overall_explanation: SOS is a serverless service that maintains its own internal data structures without user intervention. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/search-optimization-service

question: In Snowpark, what does 'Lazy Evaluation' mean?

options.text: The query is only executed when an 'Action' (like collect or show) is called.
options.explanation: Correct. Improves efficiency by optimizing the plan first.

options.text: Users don't have to write code.
The warehouse stays suspended until the user wakes it up manually.

overall_explanation: Lazy evaluation allows Snowpark to combine multiple operations into a single optimized SQL query. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/developer-guide/snowpark/index

question: Which authentication method is specifically required for users to log in via SnowSQL using a private key file instead of a password?

options.explanation: Correct. Requires generating a public/private key pair and assigning the public key to the user.
MFA is an extra layer, not a replacement for the key.

overall_explanation: Key pair authentication provides enhanced security for automated scripts and CLI tools. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/key-pair-auth

question: Which privilege is needed for a role to see the 'Query Profile' of a query executed by another user?

options.explanation: Correct. Provides visibility into all queries on that WH.

overall_explanation: The MONITOR privilege is key for performance tuning and troubleshooting other users' queries. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/security-access-control-privileges

question: Which role has the power to 'Set' or 'Unset' parameters at the Account level?
overall_explanation: Global configuration is a privilege of the Account Administrator. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/security-access-control-overview

question: Which Snowflake feature provides a 64-bit identifier that remains persistent and allows users to access a file in a stage for a specific period without expiring?

options.explanation: Correct. This is a permanent URL that doesn't expire but requires a valid session to access.
This URL expires when the session ends.
This URL has an expiration time (default 24h) but allows access without a session.
Not a standard Snowflake URL type.

overall_explanation: File URLs are permanent and require Snowflake authentication to access the data. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/data-load-snowpipe-rest-files

question: Which view shows the history of 'Login' attempts to the Snowflake account?
overall_explanation: LOGIN_HISTORY is critical for security auditing and tracking failed attempts. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/sql-reference/account-usage/login_history

question: True or False: You can use 'Snowpipe' to load data from an Internal Stage.

options.explanation: Snowpipe supports both internal and external stages.

overall_explanation: While most commonly used with S3/Azure/GCP, Snowpipe can also monitor internal stages. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/data-load-snowpipe-intro

question: Which of the following describes 'Zero-copy Cloning' storage billing?

options.text: You only pay for the unique data modified in the clone.
options.explanation: Correct. Shared data is only billed once.

options.text: You pay for two full copies of the data.

overall_explanation: Zero-copy cloning is extremely cost-effective as it only stores the delta between the original and the clone. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/tables-storage-considerations

question: What is the maximum number of days for the 'Fail-safe' period in a Permanent table?
overall_explanation: Fail-safe is a non-configurable 7-day period provided for disaster recovery. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/data-failsafe

question: What is the credit cost of 'Cloud Services' if my warehouses consumed 100 credits today and Cloud Services used 5 credits?

options.explanation: Correct. (5 is less than 10% of 100, so it is not billed).

overall_explanation: The 10% free tier for Cloud Services covers most standard accounts. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/cost-understanding-compute

question: A user wants to upload files to a stage using a Python script. Which driver/connector would they typically use?

options.explanation: Correct. Supports all Snowflake operations including PUT.
Used for loading, not uploading files.

overall_explanation: The Python connector is the standard way to automate Snowflake tasks using Python. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/developer-guide/python-connector/python-connector

question: Which function is used to convert a string that looks like a JSON object into a VARIANT type?

options.explanation: Correct. Specifically parses strings into structured VARIANT.

overall_explanation: PARSE_JSON is essential for working with semi-structured data strings. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/sql-reference/functions/parse_json

question: Which of the following commands is used to upload a file from a local Windows machine to a Snowflake stage using SnowSQL?

options.explanation: Correct. Used for uploading local files.
Used for loading from stage to table.

overall_explanation: The PUT command is the primary way to move data from a local environment into Snowflake. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/sql-reference/sql/put

question: Which function is used to check if a specific 'IP address' is being blocked by a network policy?

options.text: There is no function; you must check the LOGIN_HISTORY.
options.explanation: Correct. (Login history shows the source IP and the reason for failure).

overall_explanation: Auditing access attempts is the only way to troubleshoot network policy blocks. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/network-policies

question: A developer wants to use Snowpark Python to process data. Where does the Python code actually execute?

options.explanation: Correct. Snowpark code is pushed down and executed inside the warehouse nodes.
Snowpark is integrated into the warehouse.

overall_explanation: Snowpark uses a push-down model where the Python/Java/Scala logic runs directly on the warehouse compute nodes. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/developer-guide/snowpark/index

question: Which command is used to add a new 'Clustering Key' to an existing table?

options.explanation: Correct. This starts the background re-clustering process.

overall_explanation: Clustering keys help improve pruning on very large tables when the natural order is not efficient. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/sql-reference/sql/alter-table

question: Which Snowflake edition is the minimum required to use 'Database Failover and Failback' for business continuity across different regions?

options.explanation: Correct. Failover/Failback and higher security are hallmarks of this edition.

overall_explanation: Business Critical (and above) provides the necessary features for cross-region disaster recovery. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/intro-editions

question: What is the purpose of 'Object Tagging' in relation to 'Resource Monitors'?

options.text: To group warehouses by cost center (tag) for reporting in the usage views.
To automatically suspend warehouses with a specific tag.
options.explanation: Plausible, but Resource Monitors handle suspension, not tags.

options.text: To limit the credits used by a specific tag.

overall_explanation: Tags provide the metadata layer for chargeback and showback reporting. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/object-tagging

question: What is the function of the 'Directory Table' in a Snowflake Stage?

options.text: It is another name for an External Table.
It provides a built-in table that catalogs the files in a stage, allowing them to be queried with SQL.
options.explanation: Correct. It makes stage metadata searchable.

options.text: It lists all the users who have accessed the stage.
It stores the folder structure for the Warehouse.

overall_explanation: Directory tables allow you to use SQL to find files by size, name, or last modified date within a stage. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/data-load-dirtables-intro

question: Which view would you query to see the total credit usage for 'Automatic Clustering' in your account?

options.explanation: Correct. Tracks the serverless costs of clustering.

overall_explanation: Serverless services like clustering have their own dedicated metering views. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/sql-reference/account-usage/automatic_clustering_history

question: Which of the following describes the 'Tri-Secret Secure' feature?

options.text: A secret password shared by 3 admins.
A combination of a Snowflake-managed key and a Customer-managed key to encrypt data.
options.explanation: Correct. It gives customers more control over their data encryption.

overall_explanation: Tri-Secret Secure is part of the Business Critical edition and provides a higher level of data protection. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/security-encryption-kms

question: When creating a 'Security Integration' for SSO, which protocol is most commonly used by Snowflake?

options.explanation: Correct. The standard for browser-based Single Sign-On.
Not natively used for SSO in Snowflake.

overall_explanation: SAML 2.0 is the industry standard used by Snowflake for integrating with Identity Providers like Okta or Azure AD. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/admin-security-sso

question: A retail company wants to track 'Data Lineage' to see which views are built on top of which tables. Which view helps with this?

options.explanation: Correct. Shows the parent-child relationships between objects.

overall_explanation: The OBJECT_DEPENDENCIES view is vital for impact analysis before dropping or changing tables. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/object-dependencies

question: When a 'Time Travel' period ends for a Permanent table, where does the data go before being permanently deleted?

options.text: It is moved to a backup S3 bucket.
It is compressed into a VARIANT column.
It moves to Fail-safe for 7 days.

overall_explanation: Fail-safe is only accessible by Snowflake Support and is for disaster recovery only. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/data-failsafe

question: When a Warehouse is suspended, which type of cache is completely lost?

options.explanation: Correct. This cache is local to the compute nodes and is cleared when they are released.

overall_explanation: The local cache must be rebuilt (warmed up) every time a warehouse starts from a suspended state. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/performance-query-caching

question: What is the function of the 'VALIDATE_PIPE' function?

options.text: To review errors encountered by Snowpipe in the last 14 days.
To check if the Snowpipe syntax is correct.
To grant permissions on a pipe.

overall_explanation: VALIDATE_PIPE is used to troubleshoot files that failed to ingest via Snowpipe. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/sql-reference/functions/validate_pipe

question: How can you recover a 'Database' that was accidentally dropped 20 minutes ago?
overall_explanation: UNDROP is the first line of defense against accidental deletions. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/sql-reference/sql/undrop-database

question: What is the maximum 'Time Travel' retention period for a 'Transient' table?

options.explanation: Only for Permanent tables in Enterprise.
Correct. 1 day is the maximum for Transient tables.

overall_explanation: Transient tables are meant for temporary data and have very limited protection periods. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/tables-temp-transient

question: Which service is used to automatically scale out a warehouse by adding clusters when it becomes overloaded?

options.explanation: Adds serverless compute to a single cluster.
Correct. It adds clusters of the same size to increase concurrency.

overall_explanation: Multi-cluster warehouses solve the problem of queuing by scaling horizontally. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/warehouses-multicluster

question: In the context of 'Snowpark', what is a 'DataFrame'?

options.text: A logical representation of a data set that evaluates lazily.
options.explanation: Correct. It represents a SQL query that only runs when an action is called.

options.text: A physical table in the database.

overall_explanation: Snowpark DataFrames allow for functional programming styles that translate directly into Snowflake SQL. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/developer-guide/snowpark/index

question: True or False: A 'Consumer' of a Data Share can 'Clone' the shared database into their own account to make it editable.

options.explanation: Correct. Shared databases are read-only and cannot be cloned by the consumer.

overall_explanation: To edit shared data, the consumer must create a new table and use INSERT INTO ... SELECT from the share. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/data-sharing-intro

question: Which of the following is a characteristic of 'Secure Views'?

options.text: They can only be used in the Business Critical edition.
They prevent users from seeing the underlying SQL query using SHOW VIEWS.
They are faster than standard views.
options.explanation: No. They can be slightly slower due to restricted optimizations.

overall_explanation: Secure views are essential when sharing data with third parties to hide intellectual property. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/views-secure

question: When developing a Snowpark application in Python, where does the actual data processing happen when you call an action like .collect()?

options.explanation: Correct. Snowpark pushes the logic down to the Snowflake compute nodes.
Anaconda provides the packages, but the warehouse executes the code.
This layer only optimizes and manages.

overall_explanation: Snowpark uses push-down logic to ensure data is processed where it resides. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/developer-guide/snowpark/index

question: What is the purpose of 'Snowflake Scripting'?

options.text: To script the installation of SnowSQL.
To write procedural code (loops, variables, branches) directly in Snowflake SQL.
To automate the creation of AWS accounts.

overall_explanation: Snowflake Scripting allows for complex logic within blocks and stored procedures using SQL. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/developer-guide/snowflake-scripting/index

question: Which of the following is NOT a type of 'Virtual Warehouse' scaling?

options.explanation: This is not a Snowflake term.

overall_explanation: Snowflake focuses on Scale Up/Down (Vertical) and Scale Out/In (Horizontal). Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/warehouses-overview

question: A retail analyst wants to query a table 'at' a specific point in time from 2 hours ago. Which feature allows this?

options.explanation: Correct. Using the AT or BEFORE clause.
