overall_explanation: Reader accounts are the solution for sharing data with partners who do not yet use Snowflake. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/data-sharing-reader-create

question: Which of the following are characteristics of 'SCIM' in Snowflake? (Select 2)

options.text: It replaces the need for MFA.
It is used to move data between regions.
It stands for System for Cross-domain Identity Management.
It allows for automatic provisioning of users from Okta or Azure AD.

overall_explanation: SCIM is the bridge that keeps your Snowflake users in sync with your corporate directory. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/scim-intro

question: What is the 'Fail-safe' duration for 'Transient' tables?

options.explanation: Correct. (Transient tables do NOT have Fail-safe).

overall_explanation: Transient tables are cheaper because they skip the 7-day Fail-safe storage cost. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/tables-temp-transient

question: Which function can be used to recover a table that was accidentally dropped 10 minutes ago?
overall_explanation: UNDROP is a metadata operation that restores a dropped object instantly if it is within the Time Travel window. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/sql-reference/sql/undrop-table

question: Which scaling policy in a Multi-cluster Warehouse will 'Shut down clusters as soon as possible' to save credits?

options.explanation: Correct. Prioritizes credit saving by consolidating load.

overall_explanation: Economy mode is designed to keep clusters as full as possible and spin them down quickly. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/warehouses-multicluster

question: Which of the following can be used to monitor credit consumption? (Select 2)

options.explanation: Incorrect. This consumes credits, doesn't monitor them.
Correct. Can set quotas and alerts.

overall_explanation: Snowflake provides both real-time governance (Resource Monitors) and deep audit logs (Account Usage). Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/resource-monitors

question: Which of the following are components of the 'Cloud Services' layer? (Select 3)
overall_explanation: Cloud Services is the 'Always-on' management layer. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/intro-key-concepts

question: What is the main benefit of 'Micro-partitioning'?

options.explanation: Correct. Only necessary data is read.

options.text: It allows users to manually sort data.

overall_explanation: Micro-partitioning is the core technology that makes Snowflake's 'zero-tuning' performance possible. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/tables-micro-partitions

question: Which of the following is TRUE about 'Multi-cluster Warehouses'?

options.text: They are only in Standard Edition.
They improve concurrency for many users.
They improve the speed of a single query.

overall_explanation: Multi-cluster warehouses prevent 'queuing' when many people query at once. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/warehouses-multicluster

question: What is the maximum number of days for 'Time Travel' in 'Standard' edition?
overall_explanation: Edition selection is critical for data retention strategy. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/data-time-travel

question: Which of the following are 'Stage' types in Snowflake? (Select 3)
overall_explanation: Snowflake provides different stage 'scopes' depending on who needs to access the files. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/data-load-local-file-system-create-stage

question: Which of the following are 'Stored Procedure' languages supported by Snowflake? (Select 3)

options.explanation: Correct. The original procedural language in Snowflake.

overall_explanation: Snowflake has expanded its procedural support to include modern languages like Python and Java. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/sql-reference/stored-procedures-usage

question: Which command would you use to find out why a Snowpipe is not loading files?

options.explanation: Correct. Returns real-time execution and error state.

overall_explanation: SYSTEM$PIPE_STATUS is the first step in troubleshooting automated ingestion. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/sql-reference/functions/system_pipe_status

question: Which command is used to 'remove' a file from an internal stage?

options.explanation: Incorrect (though PURGE is an option in COPY).

overall_explanation: Proper maintenance includes removing files from stages after they have been successfully loaded. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/sql-reference/sql/remove

question: Which of the following can be used to 'Secure' a Snowflake account? (Select 3)
overall_explanation: Snowflake provides a multi-layered security model from network to identity. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/admin-security

question: Which of the following statements about 'Fail-safe' are TRUE? (Select 2)

options.text: It applies to Temporary and Transient tables.
options.explanation: Incorrect. Only Permanent tables have Fail-safe.

options.text: It starts immediately after the Time Travel period ends.
options.explanation: Correct. It is the final stage of the data lifecycle.

options.text: Users can query Fail-safe data using the AT keyword.
options.explanation: Incorrect. Users cannot access Fail-safe data directly.

options.text: It is a 7-day period for data recovery by Snowflake Support.

overall_explanation: Storage and Protection

question: Which of the following are 'Account-level' objects in Snowflake? (Select 3)
overall_explanation: Account-level objects are shared across all databases within that specific Snowflake account. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/security-access-control-overview

question: Which of the following are 'Serverless' features in Snowflake? (Select 3)
overall_explanation: Serverless features are managed by Snowflake and billed separately from virtual warehouses. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/intro-key-concepts

question: Which function is used to convert a JSON string into a 'Variant'?
overall_explanation: PARSE_JSON validates the string and prepares it for dot-notation querying. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/sql-reference/functions/parse_json

question: A retail company wants to analyze logs in S3. Which feature allows them to 'see' the files as a table without moving them?

options.explanation: Correct. Virtualized table over cloud files.

overall_explanation: External tables provide a SQL interface for data lakes without the ingestion cost. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/tables-external-intro

question: What is the purpose of 'Row Access Policies'?

options.text: To limit the number of rows returned by a query.
To mask specific columns like credit cards.
To restrict which rows a user can see based on their role or attributes.

overall_explanation: Row Access Policies act as a filter that is automatically applied to every query on a table. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/security-row-intro

question: Which of the following is TRUE about 'Dynamic Data Masking' policies?

options.text: They are applied to a specific column.
options.explanation: Correct. (Then users see masked or unmasked data).

options.text: They are applied to a table.
They are applied to a user.
They are applied to a database.

overall_explanation: Column-level security is a pillar of Snowflake governance. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/security-column-ddm-intro

question: A retail company wants to use 'Key Pair Authentication' with a 'Service Account'. Which command associates the public key with the user?

options.explanation: Correct. Stores the key in the user's metadata.

overall_explanation: Key pair authentication is the standard for secure, automated service-to-service communication. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/key-pair-auth

question: What is the 'Result Cache' retention period for a query that hasn't been run again?

options.explanation: Correct. After 24 hours the cache expires.

overall_explanation: The 24-hour window is reset every time the exact same query is run again. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/performance-query-caching

question: Which of the following are TRUE about 'External Functions'? (Select 2)

options.text: They are stored in internal stages.
They require an API Integration object.
They allow Snowflake to call APIs outside of the platform.

overall_explanation: External functions extend Snowflake's capabilities to include external machine learning or custom logic. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/external-functions-intro

question: What is 'Clustering Depth'?

options.text: The number of columns in a clustering key.
The storage size of a partition.
The number of rows in a partition.
A metric that represents how well a table is clustered.
options.explanation: Correct. Lower depth means better clustering/pruning.

overall_explanation: Clustering depth of 1.0 is ideal. High depth indicates overlapping partitions and poor pruning. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/tables-clustering-micro-partitions

question: What is 'Pruning' in Snowflake?

options.text: The process of skipping micro-partitions that do not match the query filters.

overall_explanation: Pruning reduces the I/O needed to answer a query. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/tables-micro-partitions

question: What is the default 'Time Travel' period for a 'Permanent' table in the 'Standard' edition?

options.explanation: Correct. Standard edition is limited to 1 day for all tables.

overall_explanation: Standard edition offers baseline protection; Enterprise is required for extended history. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/data-time-travel

question: Which of the following describes 'Automatic Re-clustering'?

options.text: It is only available for Temporary tables.
It is a serverless background service that maintains table clustering.

overall_explanation: Re-clustering is vital for large tables that are frequently updated. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/tables-clustering-keys

question: What is the maximum number of clusters a 'Multi-cluster Warehouse' can have?

options.explanation: Correct. This is the typical maximum limit.

overall_explanation: While 10 is the standard UI limit, larger limits can sometimes be requested from support. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/warehouses-multicluster

question: When using 'Dynamic Data Masking', what happens if a user with a masked role performs a CREATE TABLE AS SELECT (CTAS) on masked data?

options.text: Snowflake will automatically unmask the data for the new table.
The new table will contain the masked values (e.g., '****').
The new table will contain the original, unmasked values.
The operation will fail with a security error.

overall_explanation: Snowflake ensures that if you can only see masked data, you can only 'write' masked data to new objects. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/security-column-ddm-intro

question: Which function allows you to see the exact SQL text of a query executed 10 days ago?

options.explanation: Correct. Available in Information Schema (7 days) or Account Usage (365 days).

overall_explanation: QUERY_HISTORY is the primary audit trail for all SQL activity in the account. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/sql-reference/functions/query_history

question: When a Warehouse is resized from Small to Medium, what happens to the currently running queries?

options.text: They continue to run on the Small configuration until they finish.
options.explanation: Correct. The new size only affects new queries.

options.text: The warehouse waits for them to finish before resizing.
They are automatically moved to the Medium configuration.

overall_explanation: Resizing is transparent; current queries finish on existing hardware, and new queries start on the new, larger hardware. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/warehouses-overview

question: What is 'Result Reuse'?

options.text: When a user runs the same query twice and Snowflake uses the cached result.
When a result is shared via Email.
When a user copies a table.

overall_explanation: Result reuse is a 'free' performance boost provided by the Cloud Services layer. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/performance-query-caching

question: Which of the following is TRUE about 'Secure UDFs'?

options.text: They do not require a warehouse to run.
They can only be written in Python.
They prevent users from seeing the internal logic using SHOW FUNCTIONS.
They are always faster than standard UDFs.

overall_explanation: Secure UDFs (and views) are essential for sharing logic without revealing the underlying data structures. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/views-secure

question: Which of the following are TRUE about 'Dynamic Data Masking'? (Select 2)

options.text: It masks data in the storage layer.
It masks data at the time of the query.
options.explanation: Correct. Logic is applied as results are generated.

options.text: It depends on the Role of the user querying the data.
options.explanation: Correct. Different roles see different masking.

options.text: It can only be used on Numeric columns.

overall_explanation: Masking is a 'presentation-layer' security feature that doesn't affect the physical data. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/security-column-ddm-intro

question: Which of the following is a TRUE statement about 'Fail-safe'?

options.text: It is only for Business Critical accounts.
It is a 7-day period during which only Snowflake Support can recover data.

overall_explanation: Fail-safe is the 'last resort' for data recovery. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/data-failsafe

question: What is the maximum retention for 'Time Travel' in a 'Permanent' table in the 'Enterprise' edition?
overall_explanation: Enterprise Edition allows for historical analysis up to 90 days back. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/data-time-travel

question: What is the maximum retention period for 'Time Travel' in a 'Transient' table?

options.explanation: Correct. Transient tables are limited to 0 or 1 day.

overall_explanation: Transient tables trade off extended protection for reduced storage costs (no Fail-safe). Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/tables-temp-transient

question: In the context of 'Information Schema', where are the views located?

options.text: In every database within a schema called INFORMATION_SCHEMA.
options.explanation: Correct. Real-time and scoped to that DB.

overall_explanation: Information Schema is the SQL standard for real-time metadata discovery within a database. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/sql-reference/info-schema

question: What is the maximum file size recommended for optimal parallel loading in Snowflake?

options.explanation: Too small, creates too many files.
Correct. This allows Snowflake to distribute the work effectively.

overall_explanation: Sizing files correctly is a key part of optimizing data ingestion performance. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/data-load-considerations-prepare

question: What happens to the 'Result Cache' if the Virtual Warehouse used to execute the original query is deleted?

options.text: The cache is moved to another warehouse.
The cache is only valid for 1 hour.
The cache remains available for 24 hours.
options.explanation: Correct. It is stored in the Cloud Services layer, independent of compute.

overall_explanation: The Result Cache's persistence is one of Snowflake's most efficient cost-saving features. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/performance-query-caching

question: Which of the following are valid 'Privileges' that can be granted on a Schema? (Select 3)

options.explanation: Incorrect. This is a table-level privilege.

overall_explanation: Schema privileges control what objects can be created and if the schema itself is accessible. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/security-access-control-privileges

question: Which of the following are TRUE about 'Micro-partitions'? (Select 2)

options.explanation: Correct. They are never changed; new ones are written.

options.text: They store data in a columnar format.
They are managed by the user.

overall_explanation: Immutability is what allows for Time Travel and Cloning. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/tables-micro-partitions

question: How can you determine which micro-partitions contain a specific value without scanning the entire table?

options.text: By using a Search Optimization Service.
options.explanation: Correct. It uses metadata to point to specific partitions.

overall_explanation: The SOS service acts as a 'super-index' for point lookups on large datasets. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/search-optimization-service

question: Which component handles the 'Query Result Cache'?

options.explanation: Correct. It persists results globally for 24 hours.

overall_explanation: Because Cloud Services is 'always on', it can serve cached results without waking up a warehouse. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/performance-query-caching

question: In a 'Data Share', can a consumer see the 'Query Profile' of the provider's execution?

options.text: Yes, if they are an ACCOUNTADMIN.
Only if the provider explicitly grants it.
No, the Query Profile is only visible to the account that executed the query.
options.explanation: Correct. Important for security and privacy.

overall_explanation: Metadata about query execution stays within the account that pays for and runs the warehouse. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/data-sharing-intro

question: In the context of 'Snowpipe', what is the purpose of the 'STRIP_OUTER_ARRAY' file format option?

options.text: To remove the enclosing [ ] brackets from a JSON file and load records individually.
options.explanation: Correct. Required if the JSON is an array of objects.

options.text: To delete the file from the stage after loading.
To remove the headers from a CSV file.

overall_explanation: This option allows Snowflake to treat each element of a top-level JSON array as a separate row. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/sql-reference/sql/create-file-format

question: In the context of 'Snowpark', what does the 'df.select()' method return?

options.text: A new DataFrame with the specified columns.

overall_explanation: Almost every method in the Snowpark DataFrame API returns a new DataFrame object. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/developer-guide/snowpark/index

question: Which of the following are supported file formats for semi-structured data ingestion? (Select 3)

options.explanation: Correct. Supported via the VARIANT data type.
Incorrect. Unstructured data is not natively parsed.

overall_explanation: Snowflake natively parses JSON, Avro, ORC, Parquet, and XML into VARIANT columns. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/data-load-prepare

question: Which of the following is NOT a Snowflake 'Edition'?
overall_explanation: Snowflake has 4 main editions: Standard, Enterprise, Business Critical, and VPS. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/intro-editions

question: What is the purpose of the 'SYSTEM$GLOBAL_NAMES_DICT' function?

options.text: To check the status of cross-region replication names.
To provide a dictionary of reserved words in Snowflake.

overall_explanation: Be wary of functions that look official but don't exist in the documentation.

question: Which of the following can be used to authenticate with SnowSQL? (Select 3)
overall_explanation: SnowSQL supports a wide range of enterprise authentication methods. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/snowsql-config

question: Which function is used to see the credit usage of 'Snowpipe'?
overall_explanation: Snowpipe is a serverless feature and its usage is tracked in its own history view. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/sql-reference/functions/pipe_usage_history

question: Which of the following are TRUE regarding 'Snowflake Data Exchange'? (Select 2)

options.text: It only allows sharing of CSV files.
It is a private marketplace for a specific group of invited members.
It is managed by a 'Provider' who controls the membership.
options.explanation: Correct. Allows for governance within a consortium.

options.text: It is accessible to every Snowflake customer globally.

overall_explanation: Data Exchanges allow organizations to build their own curated data ecosystems. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/data-exchange-intro

question: Which of the following is TRUE about 'Clustering Keys'?

options.text: They are most effective on tables with many terabytes of data.
They should be defined on every table.
They replace the need for micro-partitions.

overall_explanation: Clustering keys should be used selectively on tables where pruning is naturally poor. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/tables-clustering-keys

question: Which of the following are components of the Cloud Services layer in Snowflake? (Select 3)

options.explanation: Incorrect. This is the Storage Layer.
Correct. Analyzes and optimizes SQL plans.
Correct. Stores partition and statistical data.
Incorrect. This is part of Compute nodes.
Incorrect. This is the Compute Layer.

overall_explanation: The Cloud Services layer is the 'brain' of Snowflake, managing everything except raw data storage and heavy compute. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/intro-key-concepts

question: Which of the following are 'Actions' in a Snowpark DataFrame? (Select 2)
overall_explanation: Actions trigger the execution of the lazy-evaluated plan. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/developer-guide/snowpark/index

question: Which of the following are TRUE about 'Reader Accounts'? (Select 2)

options.text: The Provider can restrict which data the Reader Account can see.
The Consumer pays for the compute costs.
They are created and managed by the Provider account.
Reader accounts can have their own local tables.
options.explanation: Incorrect. They are strictly for consuming shared data.

overall_explanation: Reader accounts are a 'gift' of data and compute from a provider to a non-Snowflake user. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/data-sharing-reader-create

question: Which privilege is required to see the 'Credit Usage' of a specific Warehouse?

options.explanation: Correct. Specific privilege for observing activity and consumption.

overall_explanation: The MONITOR privilege allows roles to see what is happening on a compute resource without necessarily using it. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/security-access-control-privileges

question: A retail company wants to implement a 'Data Clean Room'. Which Snowflake feature is the foundation for this?

options.explanation: Correct. Allows sharing data without moving it or exposing PII via secure logic.

overall_explanation: Account and Data Sharing

question: What happens to the 'Fail-safe' data if a table is dropped and then 'Undropped'?

options.text: It is moved to Time Travel.
It is fully restored along with the table.
options.explanation: Correct. The metadata and associated storage are linked.

overall_explanation: UNDROP restores the object's entire lifecycle state, including its protection windows. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/sql-reference/sql/undrop-table

question: Which metadata-only function returns the amount of storage (in bytes) used by a table and its clones?

options.text: None of the above (it's a view)
options.explanation: Correct. This information is found in the TABLE_STORAGE_METRICS view in ACCOUNT_USAGE.

overall_explanation: Storage metrics are retrieved from views in the SNOWFLAKE database, not simple scalar functions. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/sql-reference/account-usage/table_storage_metrics

question: In the context of 'Multi-factor Authentication' (MFA), which role is highly recommended to have it enabled?

options.explanation: Good, but not the most critical.
Correct. Due to its unrestricted power over the account.

overall_explanation: Every ACCOUNTADMIN should be protected by MFA as a mandatory security best practice. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/security-mfa

question: What is the purpose of the 'VALIDATE_MODE' parameter in the COPY INTO command?

options.text: To check if the user has permissions.
To parse the files and return errors without loading data.
To encrypt the data during transit.

overall_explanation: Validation mode is a best practice to test ingestion logic before executing the actual load. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/sql-reference/sql/copy-into-table

question: Which of the following are 'Semi-structured' data types in Snowflake? (Select 2)

options.explanation: Incorrect. This is a spatial type.
Correct. Stores any semi-structured data up to 16MB.

overall_explanation: VARIANT, OBJECT, and ARRAY are the three pillars of semi-structured storage in Snowflake. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/sql-reference/data-types-semistructured

question: Which role is the only one that can manage 'Credit Card' and 'Billing' information?
overall_explanation: Financial operations are strictly limited to the ACCOUNTADMIN role. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/security-access-control-overview

question: Which of the following describes 'Snowpipe' correctly?

options.text: It is a continuous data ingestion service.

overall_explanation: Snowpipe is designed for 'near real-time' data ingestion. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/data-load-snowpipe-intro

question: A retail analyst wants to query a 'Stream'. What data will they see?

options.text: Only the rows that have changed (DML) since the last time the stream was consumed.
The entire history of the table.
The current state of the table.

overall_explanation: Streams provide Change Data Capture (CDC) to enable incremental processing. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/streams-intro

question: In Snowpark, what is the difference between a Transformation and an Action? (Select 2)

options.text: Actions are only available in Scala.
options.explanation: Incorrect. Available in Python and Java too.

options.text: Transformations are executed on the client machine.
options.explanation: Incorrect. Both are eventually executed in Snowflake.

options.text: Transformations are lazy and do not trigger execution.
options.explanation: Correct. They just build the logical plan.

options.text: Actions are eager and trigger the execution on the warehouse.
options.explanation: Correct. Examples include collect() and count().

overall_explanation: Performance and Warehouses

question: Which of the following is NOT a characteristic of 'Zero-copy Cloning'?

options.text: It duplicates the physical data on disk.
options.explanation: Correct. This is NOT true; it only copies metadata.

options.text: It supports Databases, Schemas, and Tables.
The clone has its own lifecycle.

overall_explanation: Cloning is efficient because it avoids data duplication until changes are made. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/tables-storage-considerations

question: Which of the following functions returns the Snowflake account identifier?
overall_explanation: Essential for building dynamic scripts or identifying the environment. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/sql-reference/functions/current_account

question: Which of the following functions or views can be used to monitor 'Auto-Ingest' Snowpipe latency? (Select 2)

options.explanation: Correct. Shows credit consumption and file counts.
Correct. Shows the time files were loaded into tables.
Incorrect. This is for the Streaming API.

overall_explanation: Monitoring latency involves checking when files arrive in the stage versus when they finish loading via COPY_HISTORY. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/sql-reference/functions/pipe_usage_history

question: A retail company wants to minimize 'Remote Disk Spilling'. Which of the following is the most direct action to take?

options.explanation: Speeds up scans, doesn't solve memory issues.
Correct. Provides more RAM to keep the data local.
Handles concurrency, not memory for one query.

overall_explanation: Spilling happens when a query's working set exceeds the available RAM of the warehouse nodes. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/performance-query-warehouse-size

question: Which role is considered the 'Owner' of an object in Snowflake?

options.explanation: Incorrect. The role that created it.

options.text: The role that has the OWNERSHIP privilege on the object.
options.explanation: Correct. Privileges are tied to roles, not users.

overall_explanation: Snowflake's RBAC model ensures that permissions are managed through roles for consistency. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/security-access-control-overview

question: Which command is used to see the list of 'Stages' in a database?

options.explanation: Correct. Displays all stages you have privileges to see.

overall_explanation: SHOW commands are the standard for discovering objects in Snowflake. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/sql-reference/sql/show-stages

question: What is the maximum number of 'Fail-safe' days for a 'Permanent' table in the 'Enterprise' edition?

options.explanation: Correct. Fail-safe is always 7 days for Permanent tables across all editions.

overall_explanation: Do not confuse Time Travel (up to 90 days) with Fail-safe (fixed 7 days). Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/data-failsafe

question: What is 'Clustering Depth' used to measure?

options.text: The effectiveness of a table's clustering.
How many rows are in a table.
The depth of a JSON file.

overall_explanation: A well-clustered table has low depth, meaning filters can skip more data. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/tables-clustering-micro-partitions

question: Which function is used to get the 'Query ID' of the last query executed in the session?

options.explanation: Correct. Returns the UUID of the most recent query.

overall_explanation: This ID is often needed for troubleshooting or passing to other functions like VALIDATE. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/sql-reference/functions/last_query_id

question: Which of the following are TRUE regarding 'Internal Stages'? (Select 2)

options.text: Internal stages can be shared with other accounts.
options.explanation: Incorrect. Sharing is for data, not stages.

options.text: Each table has its own stage (@%).
options.explanation: Correct. Used for loading into that specific table.

options.text: They are hosted in the customer's own S3 bucket.
Each user has a personal stage (@~).

overall_explanation: Snowflake provides managed stages (User, Table, Named) to simplify file handling. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/data-load-local-file-system-create-stage

question: Which of the following views allows you to check if a specific 'Task' is currently 'Suspended' or 'Started'?
overall_explanation: The TASKS view in the Information Schema or Account Usage provides the current definition and status of tasks. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/sql-reference/info-schema/tasks

question: Which command is used to see all the users in a Snowflake account?
overall_explanation: SHOW USERS provides metadata about every user identity in the account. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/sql-reference/sql/show-users

question: What is the purpose of 'Private Link' (AWS/Azure/GCP)?

options.text: To share data with other regions.
To allow access to Snowflake without traversing the public internet.

overall_explanation: Private Link keeps all traffic within the cloud provider's backbone network. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/admin-security-privatelink

question: What is the purpose of 'Object Tagging'?

options.text: To categorize and manage objects for governance and billing.

overall_explanation: Tags help track sensitive data and attribute costs to departments. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/object-tagging

question: Which of the following describes 'Search Optimization Service' maintenance?

options.text: It is automatic and uses Snowflake-managed serverless compute.
options.explanation: Correct. Credits are billed to the account.

options.text: It is manual and requires a warehouse.
It only happens once a day.

overall_explanation: Like re-clustering, Search Optimization maintenance is a background service managed by Snowflake. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/search-optimization-service

question: What is the function of 'Automatic Re-clustering'?

options.text: To maintain the optimal data layout for a table as data changes.

overall_explanation: Re-clustering prevents performance degradation as new data is inserted or updated in a clustered table. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/tables-clustering-keys

question: Which of the following can be used to 'Trigger' a Snowflake Task? (Select 2)

options.text: The completion of a predecessor task (Task Tree).
A specific data row being inserted into a table.
options.explanation: Incorrect. That would be a Stream + Task combo.

options.text: A manual email sent to Snowflake.

overall_explanation: Tasks are orchestrated via schedules or dependencies within a DAG. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/tasks-intro

question: What is the maximum number of 'Tags' that can be applied to a single Snowflake object?

options.explanation: Correct. This is the current system limit.

overall_explanation: Tags allow for rich governance but have a logical limit of 50 per object. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/object-tagging

question: What is the maximum number of 'Search Optimization' paths that can be added to a table?

options.explanation: Correct. You can add it for many columns and predicates.

overall_explanation: While you can add many, each one increases the maintenance cost of the table. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/search-optimization-service

question: What is the purpose of the 'SCIM' protocol in Snowflake?

options.text: To automate user provisioning from an external identity provider.
options.explanation: Correct. Keeps users and roles in sync.

overall_explanation: SCIM stands for System for Cross-domain Identity Management. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/scim-intro

question: Which function is used to convert an 'Array' into multiple rows?

options.explanation: Creates an array from a string.

overall_explanation: FLATTEN takes a VARIANT, OBJECT, or ARRAY and explodes it into a set of rows. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/sql-reference/functions/flatten

question: Which of the following are 'System-defined' Roles? (Select 3)

options.explanation: Correct. Highest level in an account.

overall_explanation: Snowflake comes with a set of default roles that cannot be deleted. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/security-access-control-overview

question: A retail company wants to use 'Snowpark' for data science. Which of the following is NOT a benefit of Snowpark?

options.text: Logic is executed near the data.
It eliminates the need for a Virtual Warehouse.
options.explanation: Correct. Snowpark still requires a warehouse to execute logic.

options.text: It supports multiple languages (Python, Java, Scala).

overall_explanation: Snowpark is a framework, but it still relies on Snowflake's compute layer (Warehouses). Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/developer-guide/snowpark/index

question: What is 'Vertical Scaling' in a Snowflake Virtual Warehouse?

options.text: Changing the size from Small to Medium.

overall_explanation: Vertical scaling (Scale-up) provides more memory and CPU for complex queries. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/warehouses-overview

question: What happens to the data in a 'Stage' after it is successfully loaded using 'PURGE = TRUE'? Your answer is incorrect

options.text: The files are deleted from the stage.
options.explanation: Correct. Helps manage storage and costs.

options.text: The data is moved to a backup table.

overall_explanation: PURGE is a common practice to clean up landing zones automatically. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/sql-reference/sql/copy-into-table

question: Which of the following are components of the 'Compute Layer'? (Select 2)
overall_explanation: The compute layer is where the 'work' happens in Snowflake. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/intro-key-concepts

question: Which function is used to convert a JSON string into a Snowflake VARIANT?
overall_explanation: PARSE_JSON is the foundation for semi-structured data ingestion. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/sql-reference/functions/parse_json

question: Which of the following is TRUE about 'Fail-safe'? (Select 2)

options.text: It is only for permanent tables.
Users cannot manually recover data from it.
options.explanation: Correct. Snowflake Support must be contacted.

overall_explanation: Fail-safe is the last line of defense in the Snowflake data lifecycle. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/data-failsafe

question: Which of the following are TRUE about 'Network Policies'? (Select 2)

options.text: They automatically encrypt data in transit.
They can be applied at the User level.
options.explanation: Correct. Overrides the account-level policy for that specific user.

options.text: They can be applied at the Database level.
options.explanation: Incorrect. Only Account and User levels.

options.text: They can be applied at the Account level.
options.explanation: Correct. Affects all users in the account.

question: What is the 'Default' warehouse size when creating a warehouse without specifying one?

options.explanation: Correct. Starts with the smallest footprint.

question: What is the purpose of 'Resource Monitors'?

options.text: To track and control credit consumption of warehouses.

overall_explanation: Resource Monitors help prevent budget overruns by alerting or suspending warehouses. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/resource-monitors

question: What is the function of the 'Metadata Manager' in Cloud Services?

options.text: To keep track of micro-partition locations and statistics.

overall_explanation: Metadata management is what makes 'zero-management' storage possible. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/intro-key-concepts

question: Which of the following can be shared using 'Snowflake Data Sharing'? (Select 3)
overall_explanation: Sharing is restricted to read-only data objects. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/data-sharing-intro

question: Which function is used to convert a string into a date?

question: Which of the following are types of 'Caches' in Snowflake? (Select 3)

options.explanation: Correct. Stores data blocks on the compute nodes.
Incorrect (though RAM is used, it is not a named cache type in this context).

overall_explanation: Snowflake's 3-tier caching architecture dramatically improves query speed. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/performance-query-caching

question: Which of the following is TRUE about 'Stages'? (Select 2)

options.text: External stages point to cloud storage like S3.
Internal stages are managed by Snowflake.

question: Which role is required to execute the 'SYSTEM$ALLOWLIST()' function?

options.explanation: Correct. It is a utility function for connectivity.

overall_explanation: This function helps users identify hostnames and ports required for connectivity. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/sql-reference/functions/system_allowlist

question: Which of the following describes 'Zero-copy Cloning' correctly? (Select 2)

options.text: It duplicates all data on disk.
It allows for cost-effective dev/test environments.

overall_explanation: Cloning is a metadata operation that doesn't consume extra storage until changes are made. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/tables-storage-considerations

question: What is the 'Fail-safe' duration for 'Transient' tables?

options.explanation: Correct. (Transient tables do NOT have Fail-safe).

question: What does 'Horizontal Scaling' mean in Snowflake?

options.text: Adding more CPU to a warehouse.
Adding more clusters to a Multi-cluster warehouse.

overall_explanation: Horizontal scaling allows more queries to run at the same time. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/warehouses-multicluster

question: What is the maximum number of 'Fail-safe' days for a 'Temporary' table?

options.explanation: Correct. Temporary tables have NO Fail-safe.

question: Which component of the Snowflake architecture stores metadata?

question: What is the main benefit of 'Multi-cluster Warehouses'?

options.text: Improving the speed of a single complex query.
Handling high concurrency from many simultaneous users.

question: Which of the following describes 'Object Tagging'?

options.text: It allows for data classification (e.g., PII, Cost Center).

question: Which of the following are components of the 'Cloud Services' layer? (Select 3)

question: What does 'Zero-copy' mean in cloning?

options.explanation: Correct. Both objects point to the same partitions.
No, but it is initially zero until changes happen.

question: What is the maximum file size Snowflake recommends for a single file being loaded?
overall_explanation: Optimizing file size is a core part of the 'Data Movement' domain in the exam. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/data-load-considerations-prepare

question: Which of the following is NOT a valid 'Scaling Policy'?

question: What is 'Horizontal Scaling' in a warehouse?

options.text: Changing size from S to M.
Adding or removing clusters to handle concurrency.

overall_explanation: Horizontal scaling is the job of Multi-cluster warehouses. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/warehouses-multicluster

question: Which function is used to convert a VARIANT value into a numeric format?
overall_explanation: Casting is required when moving from semi-structured VARIANT to structured numeric types. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/querying-semistructured

question: Which of the following describes 'Zero-copy Cloning' correctly?

options.text: It creates a new set of metadata pointing to the same micro-partitions.
It copies data to a new storage location.

overall_explanation: Cloning is efficient because it shares the underlying data blocks. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/tables-storage-considerations

question: What is 'Fail-safe' primarily used for?

options.text: Emergency data recovery by Snowflake Support.

overall_explanation: Fail-safe is a last resort after Time Travel has expired. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/data-failsafe

question: What is the function of the 'QUERY_HISTORY' view?

options.text: To audit and monitor all queries executed in the last 45 minutes to 1 year (depending on view).

overall_explanation: Query History is vital for auditing, debugging, and cost management. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/sql-reference/functions/query_history

question: Which of the following is TRUE about 'Secure UDFs'?

options.text: They protect the internal code from being viewed by unauthorized users.
They can only be used in SQL.

question: Which of the following is TRUE about 'Clustering Keys'?

options.text: They should be used on all tables.
They are most effective on tables in the multi-terabyte range.
options.explanation: Correct. Helps with massive data pruning.

options.text: They prevent data from being compressed.

overall_explanation: Clustering should be used selectively for performance issues on very large tables. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/tables-clustering-keys

question: What is the maximum number of columns allowed in a single Snowflake table?

options.explanation: Correct. Snowflake doesn't enforce a hard column count limit beyond the row size.

overall_explanation: While there is no fixed limit on columns, the total data in a single row cannot exceed 16MB. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/sql-reference/data-types-numeric

question: Which of the following is NOT a type of 'Internal Stage'?

options.explanation: Correct. This points to external cloud storage.

question: A company needs to load 500 small JSON files every minute. Which ingestion method is the most cost-effective?

options.explanation: Correct. Designed for continuous loading of small files with serverless compute.
Cloning is not an ingestion method.
External tables provide access but are slower than ingestion.

options.text: Bulk loading using COPY INTO every minute.
options.explanation: Inefficient for small frequent files due to warehouse overhead.

overall_explanation: Snowpipe is the recommended tool for 'micro-batching' and near real-time ingestion. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/data-load-snowpipe-intro

question: What is 'Clustering Depth' used to measure?

options.text: The effectiveness of a table's clustering.
How many rows are in a table.
The depth of a JSON file.

overall_explanation: A well-clustered table has low depth, meaning filters can skip more data. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/tables-clustering-micro-partitions

question: Which function is used to combine multiple strings into one?
overall_explanation: CONCAT (or the || operator) is used for string manipulation. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/sql-reference/functions/concat

question: Which command is used to upload a file from a local computer to a Snowflake stage?

options.explanation: Correct. Requires SnowSQL or a driver.

overall_explanation: PUT is the client-side command for data movement into Snowflake. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/sql-reference/sql/put

question: Which of the following is TRUE about 'Clustering Keys'? (Select 2)

options.text: They incur costs for background maintenance.
They replace the need for micro-partitions.
They are recommended for all tables.
They significantly improve pruning on filtered columns.

overall_explanation: Clustering should be used when the natural load order doesn't provide enough performance. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/tables-clustering-keys

question: What is 'Pruning' in Snowflake?

options.text: Process of skipping micro-partitions that do not match the query filters.

overall_explanation: Pruning reduces the I/O needed to answer a query. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/tables-micro-partitions

question: Which scaling policy waits for 6 minutes before scaling up?
overall_explanation: Economy mode is cost-efficient but might lead to small wait times. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/warehouses-multicluster

question: Which function can be used to see if a 'Stream' has new data?

options.explanation: Correct. Commonly used in Task definitions.

overall_explanation: This function prevents tasks from running (and wasting credits) if there's no new data. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/sql-reference/functions/system_stream_has_data

question: A user needs to find out why a query was slow. Which tool provides a visual representation of the query execution steps?

question: In a 'Multi-cluster Warehouse', what happens in 'Economy' mode?

options.text: Snowflake waits until there is enough load to keep a cluster busy for 6 minutes.
options.explanation: Correct. Saves credits by delaying scale-out.

options.text: It only allows 1 user at a time.

overall_explanation: Economy mode is best for batch processing where slight delays (queuing) are acceptable. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/warehouses-multicluster

question: Which role is the 'Owner' of a table by default?

question: What does the 'ON_ERROR = CONTINUE' option do in a COPY INTO command?

options.text: Deletes the table if an error occurs.
Stops the load at the first error.
Skips the rows with errors and continues loading the rest of the file.

question: What is the maximum retention for 'Time Travel' in a 'Permanent' table (Enterprise Edition)?

question: What is the credit cost for 'Storage' in Snowflake?

options.text: Monthly fee based on average daily TB used.
options.explanation: Correct. Billed as a pass-through cost.

overall_explanation: Storage is calculated based on daily averages after compression. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/intro-key-concepts

question: Which of the following is NOT an 'Account-level' object?

options.explanation: Correct. It is a Database-level object.

overall_explanation: Schemas and Tables are 'children' of a Database. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/security-access-control-overview

question: Which of the following are valid 'Privileges' that can be granted on a Database? (Select 2)

Incorrect. This is a schema-level privilege.
Correct. Allows seeing the database and its schemas.
Incorrect. This is a table-level privilege.

overall_explanation: Database privileges focus on organization and access, not on individual data rows. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/security-access-control-privileges

question: Which of the following describes 'Account Usage' vs 'Information Schema'?

options.explanation: Correct. Information Schema excludes dropped and has shorter history.

options.text: Information Schema has 1 year of history.

question: Which of the following are 'Semi-structured' formats? (Select 3)

question: Which of the following are characteristics of 'Multi-cluster Warehouses'? (Select 3)

options.text: They scale horizontally to handle concurrency.
options.explanation: Correct. Adds clusters as users increase.

options.text: They automatically shut down the least-used cluster first.
options.explanation: Correct. Based on the scaling policy.

options.text: They scale vertically to handle complex queries.
They eliminate the need for micro-partitions.
They can be configured with a Maximum and Minimum number of clusters.
options.explanation: Correct. This defines the scaling boundaries.

options.text: They are available in the Standard Edition.

overall_explanation: Multi-cluster warehouses are specifically designed to eliminate queuing during high user concurrency. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/warehouses-multicluster

question: What is the maximum size of a single VARIANT column?

options.explanation: Correct. This is the row size limit.

overall_explanation: Semi-structured data in VARIANT type is subject to the row size limit. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/sql-reference/data-types-semistructured

question: What is the purpose of 'Row Access Policies'?

options.text: To restrict which rows are returned to a user based on their role.

question: Which of the following describes 'Search Optimization Service' best?

overall_explanation: SOS is like a 'super-index' for specific, highly selective queries. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/search-optimization-service

question: True or False: A user can belong to multiple 'Roles' simultaneously in Snowflake.

options.explanation: Only one is 'active' at a time, but they can have many.
Correct. A user can be granted many roles.

question: Which role is best for creating new users and roles?

options.explanation: Correct. Defined for this specific purpose.
Can do it, but USERADMIN is more restricted.

question: Which command allows you to switch to a different role in a session?
overall_explanation: USE ROLE is the standard session-level command for role switching. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/sql-reference/sql/use-role

question: Which of the following is TRUE about 'Object Tagging'? (Select 2)

options.text: Tags are used to speed up queries.
Tags can be used for cost center attribution.
Tags replace the need for roles.
Tags can be used to track PII data.

overall_explanation: Tags provide metadata that fuels governance and financial reporting. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/object-tagging

question: Which function returns the current active role?
overall_explanation: Essential for auditing and dynamic security policies. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/sql-reference/functions/current_role

question: Which of the following are 'Serverless' features in Snowflake? (Select 3)
overall_explanation: Serverless features are managed by Snowflake and billed separately from virtual warehouses. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/intro-key-concepts

question: What is the maximum retention for 'Time Travel' in 'Transient' tables?

options.explanation: Correct. Transient tables are 0 or 1 day.

overall_explanation: Transient tables are for data that doesn't need long-term history or Fail-safe. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/tables-temp-transient

question: What is the best practice for file sizing when loading data into Snowflake?

options.explanation: No, this causes 'file overhead' and slowness.

options.text: File size does not matter in Snowflake.
Files should be between 100-250 MB compressed.
options.explanation: Correct. This allows optimal distribution across warehouse nodes.

options.text: Files should be as large as possible (e.g. 5GB+).

overall_explanation: Sizing files correctly ensures that all threads in a warehouse can work in parallel. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/data-load-considerations-prepare

question: Which of the following is TRUE regarding 'Replication'? (Select 2)

options.text: It requires a Business Critical edition for failover.
options.explanation: Correct. Basic replication is Enterprise, but Failover is BC.

options.text: It allows for data to be available in different regions.

options.explanation: No, involves data egress and compute costs.

overall_explanation: Replication is the core of Snowflake's disaster recovery strategy. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/database-replication-intro

question: A company detects a 'Remote Disk Spilling' issue in a large join query. What is the most likely cause?

options.text: The table has too many micro-partitions.
The working set of data for the join does not fit in RAM or local SSD.
options.explanation: Correct. It had to spill all the way to cloud storage.

options.text: The local SSD on the warehouse nodes is full.
options.explanation: No, that would be local spilling.

overall_explanation: Remote spilling is the slowest type of execution because it involves writing temporary data to cloud storage. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/performance-query-warehouse-size

question: What happens to a 'Temporary' table when the session ends?

options.text: It is moved to Time Travel.
It is automatically dropped and deleted.
options.explanation: Correct. Only exists for the session.

options.text: It is saved as a Permanent table.

overall_explanation: Temporary tables are non-persistent and only visible within the current session. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/tables-temp-transient

question: Which role is required to see the 'Billing' tab in the Snowflake UI?
overall_explanation: The ACCOUNTADMIN role is the ultimate authority for billing and account settings. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/security-access-control-overview

question: What is 'Automatic Re-clustering'?

options.text: A serverless service that keeps tables optimized.

overall_explanation: Snowflake manages the background maintenance of clustered tables. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/tables-clustering-keys

question: A company wants to share data with a 'Consumer' who does NOT have a Snowflake account. What should the 'Provider' create?

options.explanation: Correct. The provider pays for the reader's compute.

overall_explanation: Reader Accounts allow providers to share data with anyone, regardless of their Snowflake status. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/data-sharing-reader-create

question: Which of the following is TRUE about 'SnowSQL'?

options.text: It is a command-line interface for Snowflake.

overall_explanation: SnowSQL is the preferred tool for scripting and local file operations. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/snowsql

question: A query profile shows 'Local Disk Spilling'. What does this mean?

options.text: Data is being written to S3 because RAM is full.
Data is being written to the warehouse SSD because RAM is full.

overall_explanation: Local spilling is faster than remote but slower than in-memory execution. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/performance-query-warehouse-size

question: Which role is the best for creating and managing custom roles?

options.explanation: Correct. This role is dedicated to identity and role management.
Can do it, but not the specific purpose.

overall_explanation: USERADMIN helps separate security administration from system/compute administration. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/security-access-control-overview

question: What is the purpose of the 'VALIDATE' function in Snowflake?

options.text: To check if a user is logged in.
To view the errors from a previous COPY INTO command.

overall_explanation: VALIDATE allows engineers to see exactly which rows failed in a past ingestion job. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/sql-reference/functions/validate

question: Which of the following are the three distinct layers of Snowflake Architecture? (Select 3)

options.explanation: Correct. Where data is stored in micro-partitions.
Incorrect. Part of Cloud Services/Cloud provider.

overall_explanation: Snowflake's unique architecture separates storage, compute, and services. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/intro-key-concepts

question: Which function is used to see the credit usage of 'Snowpipe'?
overall_explanation: Snowpipe is a serverless feature and its usage is tracked in its own history view. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/sql-reference/functions/pipe_usage_history

question: Which of the following is TRUE about 'Tasks'?

options.text: They can only run SQL every hour.
options.explanation: No, they can use Cron or minutes.
No (they use either serverless or a warehouse).

options.text: They can be chained together into a DAG.

overall_explanation: Tasks allow for sophisticated workflow automation. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/tasks-intro

question: What is the function of 'Snowpipe'?
overall_explanation: Snowpipe provides automated, near real-time ingestion. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/data-load-snowpipe-intro

question: A user runs a query that takes 10 minutes. They run it again 5 minutes later with no changes. Why is the second run nearly instantaneous?

options.explanation: Possible, but Result Cache is faster.
Correct. The result is returned from the Cloud Services layer without compute.

overall_explanation: The Result Cache is used when the query, underlying data, and role are identical. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/performance-query-caching

question: What is 'Result Scanning' (RESULT_SCAN)?

options.text: A way to scan files in a stage.
A way to query the output of a previous query.
A way to check for duplicates.

overall_explanation: RESULT_SCAN is useful for processing results from SHOW commands or stored procedures. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/sql-reference/functions/result_scan

question: What is the maximum number of days for 'Time Travel' in 'Standard' edition?
overall_explanation: Edition selection is critical for data retention strategy. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/data-time-travel

question: How many years of history is stored in the 'Account Usage' (SNOWFLAKE DB) views?
overall_explanation: Account Usage is the primary source for historical analysis of credit and storage usage. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/sql-reference/account-usage

question: Which command is used to add a column to an existing table?
overall_explanation: Standard DDL commands are used to modify Snowflake objects. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/sql-reference/sql/alter-table

question: True or False: Snowflake can be deployed on AWS, Azure, and Google Cloud.

options.explanation: It is available on all three major providers.

overall_explanation: Snowflake's cross-cloud capability is a major selling point. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/intro-key-concepts

question: Which of the following is TRUE about 'Tasks'? (Select 2)
overall_explanation: Tasks allow for the automation of routine data engineering workflows. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/tasks-intro

question: What happens to a 'Stream' after it is consumed in a DML statement (like an INSERT INTO)?

options.text: The data in the stream is deleted.
The stream's offset is advanced, and the changes are considered 'consumed'.
options.explanation: Correct. The stream now looks for new changes.

overall_explanation: Streams follow a 'read-and-advance' logic for Change Data Capture (CDC). Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/streams-intro

question: Which of the following are valid 'Snowpark' languages? (Select 3)
overall_explanation: Snowpark allows developers to query data using non-SQL code. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/developer-guide/snowpark/index

question: Which of the following is NOT a Snowflake 'Object'?

options.explanation: Correct. This is an external cloud object.

overall_explanation: Snowflake objects are managed within the account; buckets are managed by the cloud provider. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/security-access-control-overview

question: Which tool is required to use the 'PUT' command?

options.explanation: Correct. PUT is a client-side command.

overall_explanation: Client-side tools are needed to move data from local systems to Snowflake stages. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/sql-reference/sql/put

question: How many days of 'Time Travel' are included by default for all tables?

options.explanation: Correct. Can be increased to 90 for Enterprise.

overall_explanation: All accounts start with 1 day of Time Travel at no extra cost. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/data-time-travel

question: Which of the following is a 'Virtual Warehouse' state? (Select 3)

options.explanation: Incorrect (this is an action, not a state of a live object).

overall_explanation: Warehouses transition through these states as they manage compute resources. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/warehouses-overview

question: Which of the following is TRUE about 'Snowpark'?

options.text: It allows users to write Python/Java/Scala code that executes inside Snowflake.

overall_explanation: Snowpark brings the processing logic to the data. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/developer-guide/snowpark/index

question: Which of the following is NOT a characteristic of a 'Micro-partition'?

options.text: It is between 50MB and 500MB in size (uncompressed).
It is managed automatically by Snowflake.
It stores data in a row-based format.
options.explanation: Correct. This is FALSE; it stores data in a columnar format.

overall_explanation: Snowflake's columnar storage within micro-partitions is what enables efficient analytical querying. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/tables-micro-partitions

question: Which scaling policy is the 'Standard' policy for warehouses?
overall_explanation: The Standard policy starts clusters immediately to prevent queuing. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/warehouses-multicluster

question: True or False: A 'Masking Policy' can be applied to a column within a 'Secure View'.

options.explanation: Correct. They can be layered for maximum security.

overall_explanation: Snowflake's security features like masking and secure views are designed to work together. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/security-column-ddm-intro

question: Which command is used to see all the users in a Snowflake account?
overall_explanation: SHOW USERS provides metadata about every user identity. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/sql-reference/sql/show-users

question: What is the primary difference between 'Transient' and 'Permanent' tables?

options.text: Transient tables have no Fail-safe period.
options.explanation: Correct. This reduces storage costs but increases risk.

options.text: Transient tables are stored in RAM.
Transient tables have no Time Travel.
options.explanation: No, they can have up to 1 day.

overall_explanation: Transient tables are designed for data that is easily reproducible but needs to persist beyond a session. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/tables-temp-transient

question: What is 'Vertical Scaling' (Scale-up) in Snowflake?

options.text: Adding more clusters to a warehouse.
Increasing the size of a Virtual Warehouse (e.g., from Small to Large).

overall_explanation: Scaling up provides more memory and CPU per node to handle more complex queries. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/warehouses-overview

question: Which command is used to recover a schema that was dropped 10 minutes ago?

options.explanation: Correct. Works if within the Time Travel period.

overall_explanation: UNDROP is available for Databases, Schemas, and Tables. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/sql-reference/sql/undrop-schema

question: What is 'Micro-partition' metadata used for?

options.explanation: Correct. Contains Min/Max values of columns.

overall_explanation: Metadata is what makes Snowflake's query engine so efficient at skipping data. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/tables-micro-partitions

question: Which of the following describes a 'Standard' Scaling Policy in a Multi-cluster Warehouse?

options.text: It is only available in Standard edition.
It starts clusters only when there is high queuing.
It resizes the warehouse to a larger T-shirt size.
It starts additional clusters immediately when a query is queued.

overall_explanation: Standard policy is aggressive to ensure users don't wait for resources. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/warehouses-multicluster

question: Which function is used to extract a specific key from a VARIANT column?
overall_explanation: Snowflake uses colon and dot notation for easy JSON navigation. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/querying-semistructured

question: Which command is used to see the metadata (columns, types, etc.) of a table?

options.explanation: Shows names and owners, not columns.
Correct. Lists columns, data types, and nullability.

overall_explanation: DESCRIBE (or DESC) is the standard way to inspect the structure of an object. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/sql-reference/sql/desc-table

question: Which command is used to load data from a stage into a table?

options.explanation: Can be used but COPY is the bulk load command.

overall_explanation: COPY INTO is the primary command for efficient bulk data ingestion. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/sql-reference/sql/copy-into-table

question: What is the maximum number of days for 'Fail-safe'?

question: Which privilege is needed to 'Use' a warehouse?

options.explanation: Allows starting/stopping, not always needed for basic use.
Correct. Necessary to execute queries on that warehouse.

overall_explanation: The USAGE privilege is the most common permission granted to users for compute. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/security-access-control-privileges

question: What is a 'Materialized View' useful for?

options.text: Queries that frequently aggregate data from large tables.

overall_explanation: Materialized views store the pre-computed results of a query for faster access. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/views-materialized

question: What is the size of a 'Micro-partition' in Snowflake?

options.explanation: Correct. This is the target size.

overall_explanation: Small, granular partitions are key to Snowflake's high-speed pruning. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/tables-micro-partitions

question: Which of the following are TRUE about 'Snowflake-managed' encryption? (Select 2)

options.text: Users must provide their own keys in Standard edition.
options.explanation: Incorrect. Snowflake manages them by default.

options.text: Snowflake uses a hierarchical key model.
options.explanation: Correct. Keys are wrapped in a hierarchy.

options.text: Encryption can be turned off to save costs.

overall_explanation: Encryption is transparent and mandatory in Snowflake, ensuring data is always secure at rest. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/security-encryption

question: True or False: Snowflake allows the creation of 'Secondary' indexes on tables.

options.explanation: Incorrect. Snowflake does not use traditional indexes.
Correct. Snowflake uses micro-partitions and pruning instead.

overall_explanation: Snowflake's performance is based on metadata and micro-partitioning, eliminating the need for manual index management. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/tables-micro-partitions

question: Which of the following are 'Fail-safe' benefits?

options.text: It provides a 7-day period for self-service recovery.
options.explanation: No, only Snowflake Support can access Fail-safe.

options.text: It provides a 7-day period for Snowflake Support to recover data.

overall_explanation: Fail-safe is the ultimate safety net for catastrophic data loss. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/data-failsafe

question: Which role is required to manage 'Account-level' Network Policies?

options.explanation: Correct. Only security-focused roles can manage these.

overall_explanation: Network policies are a critical security boundary managed by high-level admins. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/network-policies

question: Which of the following are 'Time Travel' actions? (Select 2)

options.text: Cloning data at a point in time.
Querying data at a point in time.

overall_explanation: Time Travel allows you to 'go back in time' for querying or object restoration. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/data-time-travel

question: What is the 'Query ID'?

options.text: A unique identifier for every executed query.

overall_explanation: Query IDs are necessary for functions like RESULT_SCAN or for troubleshooting. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/sql-reference/functions/last_query_id

question: Which function converts a VARIANT value into a string?
overall_explanation: Casting is necessary to use VARIANT data in standard string functions. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/sql-reference/functions/to_char

question: What is 'Automatic Suspend' in a warehouse?

options.text: It turns off the warehouse after a period of inactivity to save credits.

overall_explanation: Auto-suspend is a key cost-control feature in Snowflake. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/warehouses-overview

question: Which of the following is TRUE about 'Resource Monitors' at the Account level?

options.text: They can only monitor one warehouse.
They can monitor the credit usage of the entire account.

overall_explanation: Account-level monitors help set global 'kill switches' for credit usage. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/resource-monitors

question: Which of the following describes 'Snowpipe' correctly?

options.text: A continuous, near real-time ingestion service.

overall_explanation: Snowpipe is serverless and automates file ingestion as they arrive in a stage. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/data-load-snowpipe-intro

question: What is the default compression format for Snowflake data?
overall_explanation: Users do not need to manage compression; Snowflake handles it for all data. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/intro-key-concepts

question: What is the benefit of 'Secure Views'?

options.text: Hides the underlying SQL and data from unauthorized users.

overall_explanation: Secure views are critical for multi-tenant environments and data sharing. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/views-secure

question: Which scaling policy prioritize performance over cost?
overall_explanation: Standard scaling policy is the default and aims to minimize queuing. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/warehouses-multicluster

question: Which role can view the 'Query History' of other users?

options.text: Only the user who ran the query.
ACCOUNTADMIN or anyone with the MONITOR privilege.

overall_explanation: Monitoring privileges are required to see cross-user activity. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/ui-query-history

question: Which command would you use to see all the 'Stages' in your current schema?
overall_explanation: SHOW commands are used for object discovery. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/sql-reference/sql/show-stages

question: Which of the following is TRUE about 'Dynamic Data Masking'? (Select 2)

options.text: It masks data at query time.
It alters the data on disk.
The same column can show different values to different roles.
It is available in all editions.

overall_explanation: Masking allows for 'Role-Based' data visibility without duplicating data. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/security-column-ddm-intro

question: Which scaling policy in a Multi-cluster Warehouse will 'Shut down clusters as soon as possible' to save credits?

options.explanation: Correct. Prioritizes credit saving by consolidating load.

overall_explanation: Economy mode is designed to keep clusters as full as possible and spin them down quickly. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/warehouses-multicluster

question: Which of the following are TRUE about 'External Stages'? (Select 2)

options.text: They are managed entirely by Snowflake storage.
options.explanation: Incorrect. They point to external locations.

options.text: They require a Storage Integration for secure access.
They point to cloud storage like AWS S3 or Azure Blob.

overall_explanation: External stages bridge the gap between Snowflake and your existing Data Lake. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/data-load-s3-create-stage

question: Which role is the highest level in a Snowflake account?

options.explanation: Correct. Has full control over the account.

overall_explanation: ACCOUNTADMIN should be used sparingly for administrative tasks only. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/security-access-control-overview

question: What is the main purpose of the 'Information Schema'?

options.text: To provide real-time metadata about objects in a database.

overall_explanation: Information Schema allows users to query metadata like table names or view definitions. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/sql-reference/info-schema

question: Which command is used to download a file from a stage to your computer?
overall_explanation: GET is the reverse of PUT and is used for data export. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/sql-reference/sql/get

question: Which of the following is a benefit of 'External Tables'?

options.text: They support 90 days of Time Travel.
They automatically re-cluster the source data.
They allow querying data in S3/Azure without moving it into Snowflake.
They are faster than internal tables.

overall_explanation: External tables provide a cost-effective way to explore Data Lakes. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/tables-external-intro

question: A company needs to ensure that a specific set of queries always has sub-second response times, regardless of whether the underlying data has changed. Which feature is most appropriate?

options.explanation: This is a tool, not a feature for response time.
Correct. They pre-compute results and handle data changes background, though they are best for aggregations.
Plausible, but this is for point lookups, not general query acceleration.
Plausible, but if data changes, the cache is invalidated.

overall_explanation: Materialized Views are the only object that physically stores a pre-computed result set to guarantee performance. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/views-materialized

question: Which of the following is a 'Virtual Warehouse' size? (Select 2)
overall_explanation: Snowflake uses T-shirt sizing (XS, S, M, L, XL, etc.). Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/warehouses-overview

question: Which of the following is TRUE about 'Snowpark'?

options.text: It allows processing data using Python, Java, or Scala inside Snowflake.
It requires a separate Spark cluster.

overall_explanation: Snowpark expands Snowflake's capabilities to non-SQL developers. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/developer-guide/snowpark/index

question: Which function is used to convert a JSON string into a VARIANT?

options.explanation: Plausible, but PARSE_JSON is the standard.

overall_explanation: Parsing is the first step in making semi-structured data queryable. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/sql-reference/functions/parse_json

question: What is 'Clustering Depth'?

options.text: The depth of a JSON file.
A measure of how well a table's micro-partitions are organized.
The number of columns in a table.

overall_explanation: Lower depth means fewer micro-partitions overlap, leading to better pruning. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/tables-clustering-micro-partitions

question: True or False: A 'Task' can be scheduled using a 'Cron' expression.

options.explanation: Correct. This allows for complex scheduling (e.g. every Friday at midnight).

overall_explanation: Tasks offer two scheduling options: fixed intervals (minutes) or Cron expressions. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/tasks-intro

question: What is the function of 'Automatic Re-clustering'?

options.text: It re-organizes data in the background to improve pruning.

overall_explanation: Re-clustering maintains table health as new data is loaded. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/tables-clustering-keys

question: Which of the following is a benefit of 'Secure Views'?

options.text: They allow writing data back to the table.
They hide the view definition and underlying data structure from unauthorized users.

overall_explanation: Secure views are the foundation for secure data sharing and privacy. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/views-secure

question: Which of the following is the 'Provider' responsible for when sharing data via a 'Reader Account'?

options.explanation: Plausible, but provider pays for reader's compute too.

options.text: The cost of storage AND the credits used by the reader account's warehouses.

overall_explanation: Reader accounts are 'sub-accounts' billed directly to the provider. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/data-sharing-reader-create

question: Which function is used to convert a string like '2023-01-01' into a Date type?

question: Which role can access the 'Organization' (ORGADMIN) features to manage multiple accounts?

options.explanation: Plausible, but ORGADMIN is a separate, higher role.

overall_explanation: ORGADMIN is the role for multi-account management and global billing. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/admin-user-management

question: Which privilege is required to 'resume' a suspended warehouse?

options.explanation: Plausible, but USAGE is to query.
Allows resizing, but OPERATE is for state.
Correct. Allows starting, stopping, and suspending.
Allows viewing usage, not changing state.

overall_explanation: OPERATE is the specific privilege for managing the lifecycle of a warehouse. Ref: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/security-access-control-privileges

question: Which account-level role has the 'MANAGE GRANTS' privilege by default, allowing it to modify any grant in the system?

options.explanation: Correct. This role (and ACCOUNTADMIN) are designed for security management.
